title: No Title
summary: Microsoft Elevate for Changemakers: Build AI skills for impact
llm_amount: $2,000 (USD) Azure services credits per year and a 15% discount for eligible nonprofits

[{"@type": "FAQPage", "@context": "http://schema.org", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "What technology grants and discounts does Microsoft provide for nonprofits?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft provides grants and discounts for eligible nonprofit organizations, including cloud services like Microsoft 365, Azure and Power Platform, Surface hardware, on-premises software, and digital skilling. Please note that not every program may be available in every country."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I apply for the nonprofit programs available to my organization?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "
First, review our eligibility guidelines and then get started by applying for a product grant or discount.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I get help installing and using my Microsoft products?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "
Microsoft encourages you to work with a partner in your country whose mission is to serve other nonprofits in building organization and technology capacity, such as nonprofit partners TechSoup and Tech Impact.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What training is available to learn how to use Microsoft products that I have received through a grant, downloaded online, or already have installed on my PC?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "
Microsoft has partnered with TechSoup to create the Digital Skills Center for Nonprofits, which is a collection of training courses designed to help nonprofits make the most out of the Microsoft products they already have.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Does Microsoft have a hardware grant program?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Currently, we do not have a hardware grant program. However, the resources below offer additional information as to how Microsoft and our partners provide low-cost computers and hardware to eligible nonprofits.

  • Microsoft Surface
  • Refurbished computer - submit an email to oemmar@microsoft.com
  • TechSoup hardware
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What Microsoft products and services are available in my country?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "
Microsoft provides nonprofit grants and discounts in over 200 countries worldwide. Product availability for nonprofits aligns with Microsoft commercial product availability with the exception of new Surface hardware offers which are only available in Australia, Belgium, Canada, France, Germany, Japan, Netherlands, Sweden, Switzerland, United Kingdom, and the United States. Refurbished Surface devices are only available in the United States and Canada. Our goal is to deliver purchasing experiences that offer customers the flexibility to access nonprofits offers whether they choose to buy on their own, through Microsoft, or through our certified partners—flexible options include Enterprise Agreement, Cloud Solution Providers, and Open Value Licensing. To learn more about the specific products, services, and purchasing options available in your country, complete the verification process via our nonprofit portal.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What determines the pricing for nonprofit products and services from Microsoft?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft realizes that predictable pricing is critical to strategic IT planning. To provide nonprofits transparency and help with IT planning, our approach is to provide up to a 75% discount on core products for cloud and on-premises products, and up to a 60% discount on premium products. Exact pricing may vary by purchase channel due to value added partner services and additional support levels offered to nonprofits."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What can I expect when I buy through a Microsoft partner?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "
If purchasing through a Microsoft Partner, you will be offered value added services, which can include consulting services, deployment and migration services, management services, and providing training and support for your staff. Visit the Find a Partner page to learn more.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can my library license public access devices like computer labs or laptop rentals?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "
Because many libraries provide computer training labs and other public access computing opportunities, we offer software grants that cover standard public access lab licensing options for Office Standard, Windows Professional, Project, Publisher, Visio and Visual Studio. Libraries will be able to take advantage of this program through TechSoup or can work with their existing licensing partner to purchase discounted on premises software for device-based licensing.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Which persons affiliated with the nonprofit organization are eligible to use nonprofit licenses?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Nonprofit grants are permitted only for paid nonprofit employees, and unpaid executive staff who act as leadership for the nonprofit. Nonprofit discounts are permitted for all nonprofit staff and volunteers. Nonprofit program participants, members and donors are NOT eligible for nonprofit licenses and subscriptions. Please visit the eligibility page for more details."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I license volunteers in my nonprofit?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

If your nonprofit has volunteers that you would like to invite to collaborate with your staff and employees as external guests, you can invite these external users to sign into your site and resources using a personal Microsoft account.

Alternatively, discounted offers are available if you want to provide full Microsoft 365 capabilities, including an organization email address, to your volunteers.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Are there any restrictions for volunteer licenses?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Organizations cannot share, transfer, rent, or resell nonprofit licenses or subscriptions with/to other organizations or individuals even if they are part of the same group. If the group has both nonprofit and for-profit organizations in its legal structure, nonprofit offers are only permitted for the eligible nonprofit organizations. Additionally, groups with centrally managed information technology (IT) systems are only eligible for one grant account. Please contact Microsoft to learn more about enterprise licensing options."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "My nonprofit is using Charity licenses for my staff licensing, but also provides skilling and/or education programs in our community. Can I also use student and faculty Academic licenses in my existing tenant?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Nonprofits that meet the Academic eligibility rules can purchase available Academic licenses for students and faculty, but this Academic licensing will need to be procured and managed through a separate tenant."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is there a certain usage requirement for licenses?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "To maximize the number of nonprofit organizations we can serve through our grants, Microsoft Philanthropies requires nonprofits to manage within 85% active usage of all granted Assigned Licenses. We actively review utilization of granted software and nonprofits that fail to manage within this 85% utilization requirement run the risk of disqualifying for any future grants from Microsoft."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How is eligibility for these programs determined?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft has partnered with Goodstack, a third-party validation supplier, to verify the eligibility of nonprofit organizations, public libraries, and public museums. The eligibility validation process is based on criteria provided by Microsoft and uses publicly available and locally relevant information, along with the details you submit during registration. Goodstack will compare this information against Microsoft’s nonprofit eligibility requirements. As needed, Goodstack may reach out directly to agents or organizational contacts to request additional documentation to complete the validation process."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What if a library or library system is run by a city or county government (federal or state) – do they still get nonprofit pricing?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft recognizes the critical role that public libraries play in society, and so these customers will be provided access to nonprofit pricing even if they do not hold recognized nonprofit legal status (equal to 501(c)(3) status under the United States Internal Revenue Code). During registration, please add “Library” to the Org Name to ensure seamless processing."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How long does it take to receive eligibility validation?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "We strive to make eligibility determinations as quickly as possible, and the process can take up to 3 business days. This timeframe can vary depending on nonprofit responsiveness to requests for additional documentation and country requirements."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How long does eligibility last?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Once a nonprofit organization is validated as eligible, Microsoft requires that grantees regularly complete an attestation to our donor rules to maintain eligibility. Microsoft will send a reminder when an attestation is due, but it is ultimately your organization’s responsibility to complete the required attestation. Failure to complete the required attestation may remove eligibility for Microsoft’s nonprofit programs unless/until you complete the required attestation. Additionally, you may be requested to review your current contact details and provide any required updates."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can a nonprofit submit an appeal if denied?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Please review the eligibility guidelines for your country. If you still have questions, you can contact us. Please select issue category "eligibility" and issue subcategory "why nonprofit was denied eligibility" in the form."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I access Microsoft 365, Office 365, Dynamics 365, Enterprise Mobility + Security, and Power BI?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Nonprofit organizations that have been validated as eligible can go to the Nonprofit Portal for instructions on how to purchase the nonprofit offers and assign users in the Microsoft 365 admin center."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Where do I go for support for cloud services?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Nonprofit customers should use the standard support channel for Microsoft 365. Details on Microsoft 365 support options are available on the Microsoft 365 support page."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "If I'm approved for nonprofit cloud offers, can I still purchase commercial offers?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Eligible nonprofits have access to grants and discounts that apply to Microsoft 365 commercial offers. However, there are a select number of commercial products that do not have nonprofit discounts, therefore the published prices would apply."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I access the granted Office 365 E1 licenses?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft previously provided Office 365 E1 as the primary cloud grant offer for eligible nonprofit organizations. We now provide Microsoft 365 Business Basic as our primary cloud grant offer and continue to provide grants of up to 2000 licenses of Office 365 E1 via an Enterprise Agreement. Microsoft Philanthropies also provides limited grants of additional licenses for organizations, and larger nonprofits can submit proposals for strategic grants of Office 365 E1 licenses. Contact your Microsoft account team for more information."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What are the similarities and differences between the Office 365 E1 and Microsoft 365 Business Basic licenses?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Office 365 E1 and Microsoft 365 Business Basic have the same core productivity functionality, with the primary difference that Microsoft 365 Business Basic has a 300-seat cap.

View this comparison of the features of Office 365 E1 and Microsoft 365 Business Basic for more details.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What grant requirements does Microsoft have for granted cloud subscriptions?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "To maximize the number of nonprofit organizations we can serve through our grants, Microsoft Philanthropies requires nonprofits to manage within 85% active usage of all granted Assigned Licenses. We actively review utilization of granted software and nonprofits that fail to manage within this 85% utilization requirement run the risk of disqualifying for any future grants from Microsoft."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is there a limit on the number of granted licenses that I can assign?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "To maximize the number of nonprofit organizations we can help through our grants, we actively review utilization of granted software and ask that all nonprofits manage to within 85% active usage of granted Assigned Licenses."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I see which users are inactive?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "You can see how people in your nonprofit are using Microsoft 365 services and identify who may not need a Microsoft 365 license at all. To generate activity reports in the admin center, follow these step-by-step instructions. You can export the report data to enable you to do simple sorting and filtering of all users for further analysis."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What workloads count for active usage?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft Philanthropies defines active usage as a user using at least one Microsoft 365 cloud service within the past 90 days. These cloud services include Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, Yammer, Microsoft Teams, and Microsoft 365 Apps. (Note that usage of perpetual licensed Office apps, such as Office Standard or Office Professional, does not factor into active usage metrics.)"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the difference between Total Licenses and Assigned Licenses?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "When you buy a subscription, you specify the number of licenses that you need, based on how many people you have in your nonprofit. This is the Total Licenses in your subscription management section of the admin center. After you buy a subscription, you create accounts for people in your nonprofit, and then assign a license to each person. Once a license is assigned to a user, this is the Assigned Licenses noted in the admin center, and all activity reports are calculated against these Assigned Licenses. Find more information about buying or removing licenses on this page. See an example of how Assigned Licenses are calculated here."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is nonprofit discount available for Microsoft 365 Copilot?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes, eligible nonprofits receive a 15% discount on Microsoft 365 Copilot. There is no minimum purchase requirement."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How does this new nonprofit discount affect nonprofits using the commercially priced Microsoft 365 Copilot subscription today?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Eligible nonprofit customers can switch to the nonprofit-priced Microsoft 365 Copilot subscription at renewal."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What are the eligibility prerequisites for Microsoft 365 Copilot?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Customers must have an eligible Microsoft 365 license to enable Microsoft 365 Copilot."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What applications are included in Microsoft 365 Copilot?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft 365 Copilot includes access to Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat and integrations with Microsoft 365 Copilot in Word, PowerPoint, Excel, Outlook, Microsoft Teams, Loop, and other Microsoft 365 apps. Refer for current feature availability and future roadmap."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Which languages does Microsoft 365 Copilot support?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "See the list of supported languages here."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is there a trial for Microsoft 365 Copilot?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No, there is no trial available for Microsoft 365 Copilot."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I purchase Microsoft 365 Copilot?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "If you have an active eligible Microsoft 365 subscription, you can add Microsoft 365 Copilot to your subscription as an add-on from your Microsoft admin center or get in contact with us."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the difference between ChatGPT, Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat, and Microsoft 365 Copilot?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

ChatGPT: A conversational AI developed by OpenAI, designed to engage in a wide range of discussions and answer questions on various topics. It’s a general-purpose chatbot suitable for diverse applications.

Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat: This AI-powered tool is Microsoft’s free commercial Copilot product for quickly accessing information and performing tasks across different domains. Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat offers enterprise data protection, ensuring that user interactions are secure and data privacy is maintained according to commercial standards.

Microsoft 365 Copilot: Your AI assistant for work that includes all the capabilities of Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat, with additional features specifically tailored for the Microsoft 365 suite. It assists users with productivity tasks by leveraging the Microsoft Graph for personalized and context-aware support within Microsoft 365 applications.

See ChatGPT vs. Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat: What's the difference? for more information

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Where can I find resources to understand technical readiness and user trainings for Microsoft 365 Copilot?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft is conducting end user trainings for existing and prospective users of Microsoft 365 Copilot. Trainings catalog can be found here. Learn how to use Copilot as your own AI assistant with Microsoft 365 Copilot QuickStart Training. Additionally, adoption resources can be found at Microsoft 365 Copilot – Microsoft Adoption."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What are Microsoft 365 Copilot role-based agents?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Role-based agents allow customers to enhance Microsoft 365 Copilot by connecting it to new data sources and applications and expanding its functionality. Microsoft 365 Copilot for Sales, Service, and Finance include Microsoft 365 Copilot and a role-based agent for sales, service, or finance to provide purpose-built insights and workflows."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the Microsoft 365 Business Premium nonprofit offer?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft 365 Business Premium empowers small and mid-size nonprofits to work securely from anywhere with desktop, web and mobile versions of Microsoft 365 apps (Outlook, Teams Word, Excel, and PowerPoint), enterprise-grade security and access management, and Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat for web grounding, writing assistance, and data analysis for $5.50 (USD) per user/month, paid yearly."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Who is Microsoft 365 Business Premium for nonprofit offer intended for?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft 365 Business Premium was purpose-built for small and medium-sized organizations under 300 users who need a solution that provides their employees with the best-in-class productivity, collaboration, device management, and security solutions to safeguard organization data and assets across devices."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What if I have devices that are licensed for Windows 10 or Windows 11 Home?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "You can receive a nonprofit grant for up to 50 Windows Pro licenses by registering your organization through TechSoup, please note that TechSoup charges a nominal admin fee to cover costs to operate and maintain their grant program. This nominal fee directly supports TechSoup's free informational and training content and resources available to nonprofits. You may also obtain discounted Windows Pro licenses through a Microsoft partner."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the best way to deploy Microsoft 365 Business Premium in my organization?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Partner-assisted deployment is the recommended way to deploy Microsoft 365 Business Premium. Contact your Microsoft partner and ask them if they are offering Microsoft 365 Business Premium. Your partner is well-equipped to help you understand your options and make the best recommendations for deploying Microsoft 365 Business Premium in your organization. If you do not have a Microsoft partner, you can find one on our Nonprofits partner page."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I take advantage of the nonprofit-discount if I have already purchased Microsoft 365 Business Premium?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Customers may acquire Microsoft 365 Business Premium with the help of a partner. A Microsoft partner can help modify the licenses to add the offer into your assets."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Are nonprofits impacted by the Microsoft 365 price adjustments that are going into effect on July 1, 2026?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Nonprofit pricing will be adjusted in line with commercial pricing, as it is tied to commercial rates through a fixed percentage discount. For the impacted products, the nonprofit discount rate is 60-75%. Note that all pricing is subject to change and may vary by country and currency. Learn more in the blog announcement."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is Dynamics 365?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft Dynamics 365 is the next generation of intelligent business applications in the cloud. Microsoft Dynamics 365 unifies CRM and ERP capabilities by delivering new purpose-built applications to help manage organizational functions, including Sales, Customer Service, Field Service, Operations, Project Service Automation, Financials, and Marketing through Adobe Marketing Cloud (sold separately)."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the difference between Dynamics 365 Sales Professional and Dynamics 365 Sales Enterprise?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Sales Professional provides core relationship management that is used by organizations without complex processes. For more detailed information, view our plan comparison chart. With a more basic set of capabilities, it has a lower price point of $16.25 (USD)* per user per month, which is 75% off the commercial price."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Do I have to purchase all of the apps under Dynamics 365 or can I choose what I need for my organization?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "You can choose from a selection of discounted Dynamics 365 applications, depending on the needs of your nonprofit. Visit the Dynamics 365 & Power Platform page to learn more."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is there nonprofit/charity pricing available for support?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No. But you can evaluate support options on our Dynamics 365 support page."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is Power Platform?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft Power Platform is an intuitive, extensible platform of low-code tools that empowers all users to collaborate and build transformational solutions. It includes Power BI, Power Apps, Power Virtual Agent, and Power Automate."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is there a nonprofit grant for Power Platform?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Eligible nonprofits can get Power Apps free for up to 10 seats (the “Per App” plan) and discounted pricing of $2.50 (USD)* per user per month for additional users, which is 50% off the commercial price."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the difference between Power Apps per App and Power Apps Premium?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Use the Power Platform Licensing Guide to see the differences between these two offerings. Power Apps Premium is $5 (USD)* per user per month for eligible nonprofits, which is 75% off the commercial price."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "If my nonprofit starts with Power Apps per App but then builds additional apps and needs to upgrade to Power Apps Premium, can we have 5 granted seats of Power Apps Premium?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No. At this time, the 10 granted seats apply only to Power Apps per App."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I get Power Apps per App grant in all licensing channels?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes, the 10 granted seats of Power Apps per App can be accessed through the Direct Portal, Cloud Solution Providers (CSP), and Volume Licensing channels."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I get granted seats for other Power Platform components such as Power Virtual Agent?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No. At this time, the 10 granted seats apply only to Power Apps per App."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I take advantage of the grant if I have already purchased Power Apps?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Customers may add the granted seats to your contract and environment; please work with your Microsoft partner or account team. Customers may not reduce their Power Apps license commitments to leverage the grant."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I get access to the 10 granted seats?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "
To get started on your own, visit the Dynamics 365 and Power Platform web page. Alternatively, connect with your Microsoft account team or current Microsoft Partner, or complete this form to connect with a Dynamics 365 partner who can help you take advantage of this offer.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What Azure services can I utilize with the $2,000 (USD)* credits?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Nonprofits can use the Azure credits to purchase all Azure workloads created by Microsoft (excluding Azure Active Directory, which is licensed under Enterprise Mobility and Security). You can view the whole list of services on the Azure services page."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I choose where my data is hosted?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. However, there may be a select few regions that are unavailable for this offer. For more details on locations within each region, please visit the Azure regions page."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What happens after this credit runs out?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Your subscription will convert to a "Pay-As-You-Go" offer, meaning you pay for what you use. See more details on the Azure Pay-As-You-Go page."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is this an annual credit?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. As long as your nonprofit remains eligible, you can reapply each year."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I renew the Azure Credit each year?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "30 days from your expiration date, you will receive an email with renewal instructions. Note: If you exceed the $2,000 (USD)* cap, after your complete the renewal you will need to put in a support case to move your subscription back to the sponsorship. Otherwise you will continue to be billed."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I apply this credit toward other Microsoft products?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No. The credit can only be used towards Azure services published by Microsoft."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is the $2,000 (USD)* credit split up over 12 months?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No. You can use the $2,000 (USD)* at your discretion over 12 months."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the best way to request support?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "
  1. If you have an Azure subscription and have set up your nonprofit account, you can submit a case within the Nonprofit Portal once you login. If you have not yet created an account, you can go to the Nonprofit Portal to get started.
  2. If you can’t access the Azure portal, submit a case on the Microsoft Azure support page.

If your question is related to eligibility, please contact us.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What happens at the end of the year if I have not used up my $2,000 (USD)* in credits? Do they rollover to next year?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No, but you can reapply for the offer the following year."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Does the credit cover taxes?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is it a cash grant?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No. It is an Azure credit, not a monetary grant."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is support included?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": ""Developer" Azure support is included. For substantial or significant mission critical workloads, it is recommended that organizations purchase a more advanced support plan."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Why are you asking for a credit card?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "We need a credit card on file in case you go over $2,000 (USD)*. The card will not be charged if you stay under $2,000 (USD)*."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I validate that my usage is being billed towards my $2,000 (USD)* credit from Microsoft, and not my credit card?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Go to the Azure Sponsorships page and select "Check Your Balance.""}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is ExpressRoute covered?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No. ExpressRoute is not an Azure workload but a means to connect into Azure. You will need to work directly with one of our ExpressRoute partners."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can the $2,000 (USD)* credit be used to purchase Azure Virtual Machine Reservations?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No, reservation discounts do not apply to Azure Nonprofit subscriptions."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I purchase Azure Active Directory Premium with this credit?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Azure Active Directory Premium (AADP) is separate from Azure and as such you cannot utilize the Azure grant to purchase AADP licenses.

Azure Active Directory comes in four editions: Free, Basic, Premium P1 and Premium P2. More information on license versions and pricing can be found in the nonprofit portal.

Learn more about Enterprise Mobility + Security editions

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "If we have already purchased Azure, can we apply the credit to that agreement, or will we need to have a separate subscription?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "
  • Credit card – Yes. You can merge the subscriptions through a support case. Contact Azure billing support once both subscriptions are in place.
  • Enterprise Agreement (EA), Server and Cloud Enrollment (SCE), Cloud Solution Provider (CSP), Open Volume License Program, EAP/ECI – No. You cannot merge the subscriptions.

However, while you will not be able to apply the funds to an existing subscription of this type, you can connect services deployed in your existing subscriptions to those deployed to your new subscription. This connection can either be made over public endpoints, or for services inside a virtual network via a private VPN connection.

See instructions and considerations here: Configure a VNet-to-VNet connection by using Azure Resource Manager and PowerShell.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Do I need a Microsoft Account?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Yes. If you have an existing work or organizational account, please use it to sign up for the offer (can be the same one you use for Office 365). See instructions below:

  1. Important: Create a Microsoft account or select an organizational account that will be used for your subscription. We recommend using an account that multiple people can access to (i.e azureadmin@nonprofit.org)
  2. If using an existing organizational account, ensure it has been registered with Microsoft, or register it with Microsoft on our account page.
    • You cannot use an account already being used on a separate licensing agreement (EA, CSP, etc.)
  3. Follow the instructions on the Nonprofit Portal.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Are there any restrictions or exclusions to this offer?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "By claiming your Azure grant, you agree that you will not facilitate, engage in, or participate in any use of Azure services, for crypto currency mining, denial of service attacks, spamming, or illegal activity, and/or any use that could cause harm to Microsoft or others, and will enforce strong internal controls to prevent the same. Microsoft may from time to time contact you regarding your use of your Azure services and you will upon such request provide reasonable documentation regarding your use. If Microsoft believes for any reason that such Azure services are being used for any prohibited purpose, Microsoft may, in its sole discretion, suspend or terminate your access to the Azure services."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I take advantage of the nonprofit grant for Microsoft software?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft provides software grants primarily through our partner, TechSoup. The Microsoft Digital Skills Center for Nonprofits is a collaboration between TechSoup and Microsoft that combines product training content from Microsoft with TechSoup’s deep understanding of how nonprofits use technology. We currently have over 70 courses focused on Microsoft 365 products, Teams, Power BI, Power Apps, and many more. Courses are available in English, Spanish, French, Portuguese, and Arabic.

The Microsoft Digital Skills Center focuses on “microlearning,” delivering specific bite-size portions that are easy to absorb and use. These courses can double as on-the-job support materials and all are available on demand, at a pace that fits each user’s needs.

Completion of each learning path is formally recognized through a certificate, co-branded by Microsoft and TechSoup.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How much does the training cost?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Course offerings have a nominal administrative fee that helps TechSoup, a global nonprofit themselves, support the ongoing creation, management, and delivery of the content. You can explore our to find a PC that fits your needs."}}]}, {"@type": "FAQPage", "@context": "http://schema.org", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "How is Surface different from other PCs with built-in AI?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Surface PCs are part of Microsoft’s Copilot+ PC1 family—Windows 11 devices designed with built-in AI hardware.5

A Surface PC with AI includes the following:

  • Built-in AI chip (NPU)
    Surface Copilot+ PCs include an integrated Neural Processing Unit (NPU)—a dedicated, on-device AI processor which apps may use to run AI tasks directly. When apps use the NPU to offload and accelerate AI workloads, it can support advanced experiences, like image creation and real-time translation with accelerated speed and efficiency.5
  • New Copilot+ PC experiences in Windows
    On a Surface Copilot+ PC with AI, you can get Copilot+ PC experiences1 in Windows designed to take advantage of that NPU—for example:
  1. Find and re-discover things you’ve seen on your PC more easily with Recall.6
  2. Generate and enhance images in Microsoft Photos with Restyle Image,7 Image Creator,7 and Super Resolution.8
  3. Select text to choose quick, AI-powered actions like summarizing, creating a bulleted list, and rewriting—and much more with Click to Do.9


With a Surface PC, enjoy the same Windows desktop, apps, and Surface design you’re used to—but with AI built in.1

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What can I do with AI on a Surface Copilot+ PC?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

On a Surface Copilot+ PC,1 AI is built-in to help you work, create, and connect. With a Surface Copilot+ PC, you can do all this and more with AI:

  • Get writing and research help. Use Copilot on Windows10 on supported devices to draft or refine emails and documents, summarize content, ask questions, and get ideas in supported apps and services.
  • Learn more with ease. Use Copilot Vision11 on supported devices to receive insights and step-by-step guidance based on what Copilot sees on your screen.
  • Have conversations with AI. Use Copilot Voice12 on supported devices to talk with Copilot. Just tap or click the microphone on your phone, on the web, or on your laptop or PC to begin a voice chat with Copilot.
  • Edit and create images. On supported Copilot+ PCs,1 Microsoft Photos adds AI features like Restyle Image,7 Image Creator,7 and Super Resolution8 help you enhance or reimagine your photos.
  • Improve video calls and recordings. On supported Copilot+ PCs,1 Windows Studio Effects16 can adjust framing, blur your background, and enhance audio to help you look and sound your best on calls.
  • Do more, faster. On supported Copilot+ PCs,1 Recall6 helps you find and jump back into what you’ve already seen on your PC more easily, while Click to Do9 lets you take faster action to summarize, rewrite, and more.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Are AI PCs secure?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Microsoft designs Surface Copilot+ PCs1, with security built in. Protection starts in the hardware and continues through Windows 11 features and ongoing updates.5,13

Here’s some of what helps make Surface AI PCs secure:

  • Hardware-based protection. Surface Copilot+ AI PCs1 include the Microsoft Pluton security processor14 built into the CPU to help protect credentials, identities, and encryption keys.
  • Security built into Windows 11. Features such as Windows Hello,15 BitLocker, and Secure Boot help keep your information safer when enabled and kept up to date.
  • Automatic updates. Both Windows 11 and the Microsoft Pluton security processor14 receive regular security updates through Windows Update to help maintain protection over time.


Learn about even more ways Surface devices prioritize your security.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Which Surface PCs are good for gaming?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Surface PCs run Windows 11, which supports a wide range of PC games, gaming accessories, and cloud gaming services including Xbox Game Pass.

Note: The best Surface device for gaming depends on how you play—whether you’re playing PC games locally, using cloud gaming, or connecting accessories and external monitors for a larger setup.

Here are a few Surface models to consider based on performance capability and flexibility:

  • Surface Laptop (Copilot+ PC): Designed for performance and portability, Surface Laptop is well-suited for users who value a portable gaming laptop.
  • Surface Pro (Copilot+ PC): Surface Pro’s flexible design and all-day battery life17 make it a great 2-in-1 laptop for gaming at home or on the go.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I choose the best Surface Copilot+ PC for gaming and streaming?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Choosing a Surface Copilot+ PC for gaming and streaming depends on how you plan to use it—whether you game and stream separately, do both at the same time, or switch between different setups.

When comparing streaming and gaming PCs, consider:

  • How you plan to use gaming and streaming: Some players stream gameplay live, while others use streaming services to play games, watch content, or game casually. Understanding your usage helps narrow the right balance of performance and flexibility.
  • Support for accessories and monitors: Both gaming and streaming often involve external monitors, controllers, microphones, webcams, and other accessories. A PC that supports standard peripherals makes it easier to customize or expand your setup.
  • Connectivity and reliability: Stable connections and modern ports are important for both gaming and streaming, especially when using multiple accessories or external displays.
  • Flexibility in gaming options: Many players use a mix of local PC gaming and cloud gaming. Choosing a Windows PC that supports both gives you more ways to play and stream content.
  • Form factor and space: Whether you prefer a portable PC or a more stationary setup can influence what works best for gaming and streaming in your space.


For many users, the best PC for gaming and streaming is one that supports Windows gaming, works with common accessories and monitors, and adapts to different ways of playing and watching. Comparing devices side by side can help you find the option that fits your needs now and as they evolve. 

Surface Copilot+ PCs are designed for productivity and AI-powered experiences, but they can absolutely play games too. Many popular titles run well on Surface, especially with features like Auto Super Resolution that use on-device AI to enhance your gameplay. They're a great fit if you enjoy gaming as part of a well-rounded PC experience — catching up on your favorite games between classes or meetings, playing on the go with outstanding battery life, or streaming games via cloud gaming services like Xbox Cloud Gaming.

", "h4", {"text": 1683, "balanceDisabled": 176, "level": 270, "appearanceLevel": 650, "alignment": 1684, "weight": 277, "applyActionToContainer": 176, "type": 273}, "

icon Know the risks

", "start", {"text": 1686, "balanceDisabled": 176, "level": 270, "appearanceLevel": 1687, "alignment": 1684, "applyActionToContainer": 176, "type": 273}, "

Actions in Edge Preview is experimental. Please review results carefully and use with caution. Copilot will do its best to recognize when to trigger an action. While it’s designed to help you get things done, it may occasionally misinterpret actions. Be mindful when asking Copilot to perform an action that involves sensitive or confidential information.

", "xs", {"text": 1689, "balanceDisabled": 176, "level": 270, "appearanceLevel": 650, "alignment": 1684, "weight": 277, "applyActionToContainer": 176, "type": 273}, "

icon Understand how your data is used

", {"text": 1691, "balanceDisabled": 176, "level": 270, "appearanceLevel": 1687, "alignment": 1684, "applyActionToContainer": 176, "type": 273}, "

", {"text": 1693, "balanceDisabled": 176, "level": 270, "appearanceLevel": 650, "alignment": 1684, "weight": 277, "applyActionToContainer": 176, "type": 273}, "

icon Stay vigilant online

", {"text": 1695, "balanceDisabled": 176, "level": 270, "appearanceLevel": 1687, "alignment": 1684, "applyActionToContainer": 176, "type": 273}, "

", {"text": 1728, "appearanceLevel": 1264, "applyActionToContainer": 176, "type": 273}, "

Great for handling your everyday tasks like ordering groceries, formatting content, and shopping.

Manage permissions, clear history, and adjust settings whenever you want. Privacy features like context clues, video highlights, and memory are optional—turn them on or off anytime. Your data stays private and follows Microsoft's trusted standards.

"What's the difference between Xbox Series X and Xbox Series S?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "
  • Both Xbox Series X|S consoles deliver next-generation capabilities powered by the Xbox Velocity Architecture, such as faster loading, the ability to seamlessly switch between multiple games with Quick Resume, richer and more dynamic worlds, and frame rates up to 120 FPS.
  • The primary difference between the two consoles is that the Xbox Series X displays games in native 4K, is available with a UHD Blu-ray Disc player or as a Disc-free console, and you can choose between 1TB SSD or 2TB SSD. Xbox Series S is designed for disc-free gaming at 1440p (with the ability to upscale to 4K) and you can choose between 512GB SSD or 1TB SSD. And, of course, they're priced differently. Both consoles have the option to expand available custom storage via the Storage Expansion Card. Compare Xbox consoles.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Which accessories are compatible with Xbox Series X|S?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Good news—a number of Xbox (including Xbox One) and third-party accessories such as controllers and headsets are compatible with Xbox Series X|S Learn more about compatible accessories or shop Xbox accessories here."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I connect my Xbox controller to PC?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Many PC games let you play with an Xbox Wireless Controller instead of a keyboard or mouse.

You can connect your controller to a Windows device by using a USB cable, the Xbox Wireless Adapter for Windows or Bluetooth. Some Windows devices also come with Xbox Wireless functionality built in so that you can connect a controller directly without an adaptor. Shop controllers here.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Which Xbox consoles play discs?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Xbox Series X (in carbon black) as well as Xbox Series X – 2 TB Galaxy Black Special Edition come equipped with a UHD Blu-ray Disc player."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I add warranty to a controller or Xbox console?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "
  • The easiest way to make sure you’re covered from the get-go is to add Microsoft Complete when you buy a new Xbox console, Xbox Wireless Controller or Xbox Elite Wireless Controller. You can also get Microsoft Complete by contacting one of our support service agents. Contact Microsoft Store Support
  • Already have Microsoft Complete? We can help with everything from registering your device to coverage status and expert technical support. Check Coverage Status
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": " What games can I play on an Xbox Series X|S console?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Xbox Series X|S consoles are compatible with thousands of games across four generations of Xbox. And, with Smart Delivery games, you can buy a digital game once and get the best version of that game for the console you’re playing on. To play a backwards compatible game, insert the disc or search your library and select the game you want to play. Learn more about compatible games and shop for games directly from your Xbox Series X console or online at Xbox.com."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is a certified refurbished console?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "The Microsoft Certified Refurbished program allows a pre-owned Xbox game console to be offered at a discounted price. These previously used game consoles undergo a complete refurbishment process before being offered for sale. Refurbished consoles are available exclusively at Microsoft Store."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": " How do I request a refund for a digital game?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

We understand that sometimes purchases of digital game products don’t go as planned. When that happens, we’ll help you request a refund. Refunds are typically issued for requests meeting the following criteria:

  • Requests are made within 14 days of the purchase date
  • Requests are for games in which you haven’t accumulated a significant amount of play time

Learn more here.

"mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the Microsoft Store Trade-in Program powered by Teladvance?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

The Microsoft Store Trade-in Program powered by Teladvance lets you trade in an eligible used device for cash payment. Just answer a few questions about the brand, model, and condition of your used device. Teladvance provides an appraisal that reflects a fair estimate of your device’s condition and value. Additional trade-in promotions that we may offer from time to time may require the purchase of a new qualifying device from Microsoft Store prior to trade-in. For these promotions, you’ll be asked to upload a proof-of-purchase PDF.

If you approve of the trade-in value, create a Teladvance account. Then, print the prepaid shipping label and ship your used device to Teladvance. Once your used device is received, inspected, and approved, Teladvance will issue a cash payment to you within 14 days.

If your used device isn’t eligible for trade-in, you can still recycle it through Microsoft’s recycling resources in your region.

Read the full Microsoft Store Trade-in Program terms and conditions.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I get paid after completing my online trade-in?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Once your used device is received, inspected, and approved, Teladvance will issue a cash payment via your choice of PayPal or bank transfer within 14 days. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "I just bought a new device at Microsoft Store that qualifies for a trade-in promotion. How do I participate in this promotion?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Promotions are limited-time offers that apply only to select devices purchased online at Microsoft Store. To participate, you must purchase a qualifying device first and then proceed with the trade-in process of an eligible used device.

There are some extra steps to participate in trade-in promotions. During the trade-in process for your used device, you’ll need to answer questions about your new device. If your new device qualifies for a trade-in promotion, you’ll be asked to upload a proof of purchase.

Your proof of purchase is a PDF version of your order details from Microsoft Store. Once you’ve received a notification that your new device has shipped, follow these steps:

  1. Sign in to your Microsoft account.
  2. Go to Order history.
  3. Locate the order of your new device.
  4. Select Details to view and save the order details as a PDF—this is your proof of purchase.
  5. Go to the Teladvance trade-in page to upload your proof of purchase and get a trade-in value.
     

For more help, see the proof-of-purchase steps.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "I participated in a trade-in promotion for a pre-order device. When should I start the trade-in process?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "For pre-orders, wait to start the trade-in process until your payment method is charged and the new device has shipped. If the trade-in requires you to submit the International Mobile Equipment Identity (IMEI) number of the new device, you’ll need to wait until you receive the new device. See Microsoft Support pages for help with pre-orders."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Where can I trade in my laptop?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "You can trade in your laptop through the Microsoft Store Trade‑In Program powered by Teladvance (US and CA). To find out how much your laptop is worth, simply choose Get a trade‑in value on the Microsoft Trade‑In page and follow the prompts to get started with your laptop trade‑in."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I prepare a laptop for a trade-in?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Before your laptop trade‑in, back up your data with Windows Backup and reset your device to factory settings to remove personal info. Include original charger, accessories, and hard drive in the package. Be sure to ship it within 15 days of approval using the shipping label provided by Teladvance (US and CA)."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I trade in my Microsoft Surface or other laptop?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "You can trade in eligible Microsoft Surface devices or other eligible Microsoft laptop models via the Microsoft Store Trade‑In Program. Just check your device’s eligibility and find your trade-in value."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I trade in my Xbox One for an Xbox Series X|S?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "You can trade in your Xbox One (or Xbox One S) through the Microsoft Trade‑In Program for cash back. If you have purchased a new Xbox Series X or S, you may be eligible for higher payments for your trade-in - especially during trade‑in promos. Visit the Trade‑In page, check eligibility, and you may qualify for up to $180 cash back."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How much can I trade in my Xbox One or Series S for?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Your Xbox One or Series S trade-in value varies based on device type and condition. The final amount is determined by Teladvance (US and CA) following inspection. For Xbox console promotions, you can get up to $180 cash back, but exact amounts depend on model and condition."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Where can I trade in my Xbox?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "You can trade in your Xbox through the Microsoft Store Trade-In Program. Check your eligibility and receive an estimate through the Xbox trade-in value tool. Within 15 days after you receive your shipping label, send your console to Teladvance (US and CA), and you will receive payment via PayPal or bank transfer once it is approved."}}]}, {"@context": play disc games on Xbox Series S or Xbox Series X?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "To play a physical disc on Xbox Series X, insert the disc or search your library and select the game you want to play. However, the Xbox Series S is an all-digital gaming console for disc-free gaming. Disc-based games are not compatible with the Xbox Series S."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Does the Xbox Series X have backwards compatibility?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Xbox Series X is backwards compatible with thousands of games across four generations of Xbox. With Smart Delivery games, you can buy a game once and get the best version of that game for the console you’re playing on. To play a backwards compatible game, insert the disc or search your library and select the game you want to play."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Does the Xbox Series S have backwards compatibility?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Xbox Series S is also backwards compatible with thousands of games across four generations of Xbox. With Smart Delivery games, you can buy a digital game once and get the best version of that game for the console you’re playing on. Because Xbox Series S is all-digital, to play a backwards compatible game just access your digital game library through the console and select the game you want to play."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Which games are included as Xbox Smart Delivery games?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "All Xbox Game Studio titles include Smart Delivery, so you get the best version of the game no matter which console you play on. Partner games that have chosen to include Smart Delivery will make note of the feature on their digital game page or on the box the disc comes in."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do you download games on the Xbox Series S?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Your games need to be downloaded to your device whether they’re physical or digital, and they take up the same amount of space. However, digital games give you more flexibility, like remote installation and pre-loading before games launch. Learn more about buying digital games on Xbox and remotely installing and managing games here.

More and more games are also using FastStart technology that allows you to start the game twice as fast while the rest of the game finishes downloading. Get more specific details from Support."}}]}, Microsoft is committed to providing its customers with software that has been tested for safety and security. Although no system is completely secure, we use processes, technology, and several specially focused teams to investigate, fix, and learn from security issues to help us meet this goal and to provide guidance to customers on how to help protect their PCs.

As part of the Microsoft software release process, all software released to the Download Center is scanned for malware before it is made available for public download. Additionally, after release, the software available from the Download Center is routinely scanned for malware. Microsoft recognizes that the threat environment is constantly changing and will continue to evolve over time, and we are committed to process improvements that will help protect our customers from malware threats

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I keep my software up to date?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft offers a range of online services to help you keep your computer up to date. Windows Update finds updates that you might not even be aware of and provides you with the simplest way to install updates that help prevent or fix problems, improve how your computer works, or enhance your computing experience. Visit Windows Update to learn more."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What can I find in the Microsoft Download Center, and how do I find what I’m looking for?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

The Microsoft Download Center has recently been revised to better serve you as a one-stop shop for products available for purchase, in addition to products and downloads available for free. For your convenience, items available for purchase are linked directly to Microsoft Store. Items available as free downloads are linked to details pages, where you can learn more about them and initiate downloads.

If you would like Microsoft to scan your computer and recommend updates, please see the preceding question. If you would like to search for specific downloads, start by using one of these methods.

  • To find products and downloads, in the navigation bar that appears across the center of the Download Center home page, click an icon.
  • To search more specifically for a product or download, on the menu bar displayed near the top of the page, click a menu name and then click a product or download.
  • To search by keyword, at the top of the page, in the Search Download Center search box, type the word or words you wish to search for.


On the Search results page, you may see the following:

  • An Editor's choice listing of products and downloads, which are recommendations based on the search term.
  • A Sort by box on the right side of the page. Click the down arrow, and select a display order for the search results.
  • A list of Filter options on the left side of the page. Click a Filter option to narrow the list of items displayed in the search results. The Filter option operates together with the original search term to narrow the scope of search results.


When you see the item you’re interested in, click the item link to find out more about downloading or purchasing it.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I find worldwide downloads?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Microsoft delivers downloads in more than 118 languages worldwide. The Download Center now combines all English downloads into a single English Download Center. We no longer offer separate downloads for U.S. English, U.K. English, Australian English, or Canadian English.

Select a Worldwide download from the English-language site

  1. On the download details page for a particular item, under Quick details, you will see a Change language box. Select the desired language from the drop-down list

Note: The Change Language box appears only when a worldwide version of the download is available. If the box does not appear, the download has not yet been made available in languages other than English. If you see the box, but a specific language is not listed, the download is not yet available in that language.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Which other Microsoft websites offer downloads?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

In this section, you will find links to other Microsoft websites that offer downloads. Note that almost all of the downloads available from these websites can also be found in the Microsoft Download Center; however, the other Microsoft sites may offer services and information that you may not find in the Download Center.

If you are looking for downloads for the Windows operating system or for Microsoft Office, try using Windows Update, which helps you keep your computer up to date without requiring you to visit the Download Center.

  • DirectX downloads

    Get the latest version of Microsoft DirectX, and find other important DirectX updates for your version of Windows.

  • Service Packs for Microsoft Products

    Download service packs containing updates for system reliability, program compatibility, and security. See which service packs are currently available from Microsoft Help and Support.

  • Windows Media Download Center

    Amplify your play with Microsoft Windows Media Player, encoders, codecs, and other related downloads.

  • Azure downloads

    Get SDKs and tools for managing and interacting with Azure services

  • Windows developer resources

    Get downloads and tools for Windows development

  • Code samples

    Get started with Microsoft developer tools and technologies. Explore our samples and discover the things you can build.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What should I do if I can’t find what I am looking for?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "If you cannot find a specific download, it may be available from a company other than Microsoft. Popular examples include Adobe Reader, Macromedia Shockwave and Flash players, and Java software. For current links to these downloads on their respective websites, use Bing to search the web for the download you are looking for. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What information will I find on download pages?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

When you click a download item in the Download Center, you will be directed to the download details page for that download. Most of these pages follow a standard format and include most of the sections described here.

  • Quick details

    The Quick details section contains helpful summary information, including the name and size of the download file and its date of publication.

  • Change language

    In some cases, you'll see a Change language box, from which you can select a language other than English. Note that some downloads are not available in all languages. In addition, not all non-English downloads are accompanied by localized details pages. As a result, you may find English descriptions and instructions on pages for non-English downloads.

  • Download button or link

    For each file listed, you will see a Download button or link in the Quick details section. Click this button or link to begin the download.

  • Overview

    The Overview section includes a brief explanation of the issues addressed by the download and the benefits it provides.

  • System requirements

    Always check the System requirements section to make sure your computer is compatible with and has the space required to install and run the download. This section may also list required or recommended application software.

  • Instructions

    Review the Instructions section before clicking the Download button, so you are prepared for any steps you may need to follow.

  • What others are downloading

    This lists other items downloaded by people who also downloaded the current item. Information about these related downloads can be very helpful.

  • Additional information

    This provides further information that is either directly related to the current download or is meant to help keep your system running securely, such as security update information.

  • Related resources

    These links take you to how-to information, newsgroups, special offers, and other resources pertinent to the download you have selected.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What should I do if I cannot complete a download?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "If you cannot complete a download, you may need to clear the cache in your browser.  Here are Instructions for how to clear the cache in Microsoft Edge."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I install downloaded software?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Before you can use any software that you download, you must install it. For example, if you download a security update but do not install it, the update will not provide any protection for your computer. Follow these steps to save and install a download:

  1. On the download details page, click the Download button.
  2. A dialogue box will open. Click the Save button.
  3. You will be prompted to select a location for the download on your computer. Select an appropriate location, and click the Save button.
  4. When the file has finished downloading, you will see a second dialog box. If you want to install the file immediately, click the Open button. This launches an installation wizard.
  5. If you do not want to immediately install the file, click the Close button. When you are ready to install the file, double-click the file to launch the installation wizard.

Note: When you save a download file, make sure to save it to a location that you will remember when you are ready to install the download. We suggest creating a special folder on your desktop to which you always save downloads. Follow these steps to create such a downloads folder:

  1. Right-click an unused area of your desktop.
  2. Point to New, and then click Folder.
  3. Give the folder a name, such as My Downloads.
"}}]}, {"cookieMaxAgeSeconds": 1800, "isInScope": false, "targetModes": ["targetPageParamsAll", "getOffer"], "targetMboxName": "target-global-mbox"}, {"@type": "FAQPage", "@context": "http://schema.org", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "What can I purchase with a Microsoft gift card?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "With a Microsoft gift card, you can buy apps, games, movies, and more from the Microsoft Store, on Windows PCs, and on Xbox. You can also use it to purchase subscriptions like Xbox Game Pass."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What can I purchase with an Xbox gift card?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "With an Xbox gift card, you can buy games, add-ons, in-game content, apps, movies, and TV shows on Xbox consoles and the Microsoft Store. You can also use it toward subscriptions like Xbox Game Pass."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Where can I buy a Microsoft or Xbox gift card?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "You can buy Microsoft and Xbox gift cards online from the Microsoft Store or at participating retailers. Digital gift cards can be delivered instantly by email, making them a convenient option for gifting."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do you gift Xbox Game Pass?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Where can you use an Xbox or Microsoft gift card?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Xbox and Microsoft gift cards can be used on the Microsoft Store online, on Xbox consoles, and on Windows PCs.
