The recipient will receive a code they can redeem on their account."}}]}, {"cookieMaxAgeSeconds": 1800, "isInScope": false, "targetModes": ["targetPageParamsAll", "getOffer"], "targetMboxName": "target-global-mbox"}, {"@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "FAQPage", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "What is Microsoft Copilot?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Microsoft Copilot is your digital companion designed to inform, entertain, and inspire. Using advanced AI, Copilot understands your questions and requests, provides direct answers, assists with writing, and even creates images. It's like having a smart, helpful friend at your fingertips!

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What can I do with Copilot?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Whether you start with a question or a request, Copilot can give advice, feedback, straightforward answers, and creative help:

  • Get answers: Got a burning question or need info on a specific topic? Ask Copilot, and you'll get a direct response.

  • Conduct research: Need the latest information or a quick summary on a subject? Copilot can search the web and provide concise summaries with sources.

  • Have engaging conversations: Whether you're looking to chat about a topic, need a non-judgmental conversation partner, or simply want to vent, Copilot is here to engage in meaningful discussions.

  • Enhance language skills: Need help with translations, learning a new language, or Improving your writing? Just ask Copilot for help.

  • Boost creativity: Whether it's brainstorming for a project, crafting a social post or email, or creating an image, Copilot can help you kickstart and polish your work.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How should I interact with Copilot?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

To get started, simply type your message and send it. Alternatively, in select markets, you can click the microphone button and speak your mind.

To improve your overall experience, consider trying the following:

  • Use natural language and interact as if you're talking with a friend. The more you converse naturally, the better Copilot can understand your needs and provide tailored assistance. This approach helps Copilot learn your preferences and deliver an even more engaging and helpful experience throughout your conversations.

  • Ask questions and follow up. Copilot provides direct answers and remembers your follow-up questions to give meaningful replies. Whether you want to learn more, reframe the answer, or question the response, Copilot will respond with context in mind.

  • Ask Copilot to rephrase responses. Just say something like, “Can you explain that more simply?”, “break that down in simpler terms”, or “explain that like I’m a beginner” and Copilot will reply with an answer that’s easier to understand.

  • Ask for different formats. Would you prefer to see an answer as a list or table? Just ask.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How old do I have to be to use Copilot?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Copilot is available to customers over the age of 18. We have also expanded access to users under 18 subject to minimum age requirements that vary by country or region. Copilot is inaccessible to users under the ages listed here (note: these restrictions apply regardless of parental consent or account management). If your country or region is not listed, then the minimum age requirement is 13.

Additional age restrictions may apply to new or experimental features, including those released through Copilot Labs or to Microsoft 365 Premium subscribers.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What languages does Copilot support?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Copilot will automatically recognize the language you’re using and respond accordingly. Information on currently-supported languages is available at Supported languages for Microsoft Copilot.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What’s the difference between Copilot and Microsoft 365 Premium?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Copilot provides a free baseline experience, while Microsoft 365 Premium, a paid subscription, unlocks powerful AI1 tools, productivity apps, and advanced security. Premium subscribers get the highest usage limits for Copilot features, exclusive access to advanced AI capabilities, and apps like Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook with Copilot built in. You also get up to 6 TB of cloud storage (1 TB per user), Microsoft Defender, and AI-powered tools like Designer and Clipchamp—all in one plan.

To learn more about the Copilot and Microsoft 365 Premium experiences, visit the Microsoft 365 Premium page.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I access Copilot?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

You can access Microsoft Copilot:

  • Online at https://copilot.microsoft.com

  • On your phone via the Android or iOS mobile app

  • On your Mac with the Copilot app

  • On your Windows PC with the Copilot app

  • On your Edge browser by clicking the Copilot icon on the top right

  • In your Xbox Mobile App and Game Bar for Windows 11

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is Copilot Labs and how do I access it?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Copilot Lab features are experimental AI initiatives. We invite you to stay up to date on our latest experiments and learn more here.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is Copilot on Windows?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Copilot on Windows is your AI assistant built into the PC experience, helping you quickly find information, adjust settings, and get tasks done with ease. Learn more at Microsoft Support.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the Copilot on Windows wake word?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Copilot on Windows users can invoke Copilot with the phrase “Hey Copilot”. This opt-in feature gives you a new way to easily start a conversation with Copilot Voice, anytime the feature is enabled, your PC is unlocked, and the Copilot app is running. End your conversation by tapping the X on the Copilot Voice interface. With this new hands-free experience, you can stay in your flow when you need answers to a question or just need someone to bounce an idea off of.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Where can I use the Copilot wake word?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Today, the wake word, “Hey Copilot”, is only available on the Copilot on Windows app.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is “Hey Copilot” on by default?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

No, you must enable this feature within Copilot’s settings to use it.

To configure click here or:

  1. Open Copilot
  2. Tap your avatar at the bottom of your sidebar
  3. Tap Settings
  4. Scroll down to Voice mode
  5. Toggle “Listen for ‘Hey Copilot’ to start a conversation on or off”. It is off by default.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I use “Hey Copilot” once enabled?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

  • Anytime your PC is unlocked, the feature is enabled, and the Copilot app is running, simply say “Hey Copilot” or “Hey Copilot, what is the weather in Palm Springs this weekend?” (or whatever question you have) and Copilot Voice will respond.

  • Anytime the wake word is detected you will see the Copilot Voice Floating UI appear on the bottom of your screen and either hear a small chime, or a voice greeting or response from Copilot.

You can end a conversation by tapping the X on the Copilot Voice interface or wait a few seconds without speaking and the call will end automatically. The Copilot Voice interface will disappear when the call has ended.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Why does Windows show my microphone in use when I turn on “Hey Copilot”?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Windows will show Copilot in use of the microphone via the Windows System Tray when either of these are true:

  • “Hey Copilot” is enabled and the Copilot app is running in (open, minimized, or running in the background)

  • Any Copilot Voice conversation is going on

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I tell the difference between if Copilot is only listening for the wake word or if I’m in a Copilot Voice conversation?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

If you see the Copilot Voice interface, you are in a conversation with Copilot Voice.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is my data/voice/recordings sent to the cloud when “Hey Copilot” is enabled?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

No. When “Hey Copilot” is enabled, Copilot uses the microphone with an on-device wake word spotter that only detects the “Hey Copilot” phrase.

The wake word spotter uses an on-device 10 second audio buffer in memory. This audio buffer is never recorded or stored locally.

When the wake word is detected, the Copilot Voice Floating UI will appear and begin to establish a Copilot Voice conversation. In parallel, the audio buffer will send audio from when “Hey Copilot” was detected to the cloud so it can be used to help answer the question you asked.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Will “Hey Copilot” work when my PC is off or locked?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

No, your PC must be powered on and unlocked. It will respond to any voice it hears when unlocked.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Will “Hey Copilot” work if I am offline?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Recognition of the wake word is always performed locally and will try to establish a Copilot Voice call when detected. If you're offline, Copilot will attempt to establish a connection but won’t succeed, and the call will end. Copilot Voice responses require internet access and cloud processing, similar to typed interactions.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Will having “Hey Copilot” enabled impact the battery life on my PC?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

While “Hey Copilot” is optimized to work on a broad variety of devices, some users may find impact on their battery when using this feature. Impact can vary depending on battery size, system configuration, and environment. Users should verify they have the latest updates from Windows Update.

If users find their battery life experience to be high, users can disable “Hey Copilot” and use other methods like Press to talk to quickly access Copilot Voice.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Will audio quality be impacted if I use a Bluetooth headset?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Some Bluetooth headsets may experience reduced audio quality playback when “Hey Copilot” is enabled. Users can check with their headset vendor for latest firmware or drivers which may address the problem. If the issue persists, users can disable “Hey Copilot” and may prefer to use Press to talk to quickly access Copilot Voice instead.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What languages work with “Hey Copilot”?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

While this feature is available worldwide, the “Hey Copilot” wake word is only trained and available in English. After starting a conversation, you can continue speaking in any language that is currently supported.

"}}]}, {"@type": "FAQPage", "@context": "http://schema.org", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿﻿What is the Microsoft Store returns policy?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "You can return almost any physical product purchased online from Microsoft Store for a full refund within 60 days from when you receive the product – or through January 31, 2026 with our Holiday extended return policy for eligible physical purchases made between October 1, 2025 – December 2, 2025 in select markets – , as long as you return the product in like-new condition in its original packaging, along with all parts, components, instruction manuals, and documentation that were initially included. "}}]}, {"@type": "FAQPage", "@context": "http://schema.org", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "How is Surface different from other PCs with built-in AI?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Surface PCs are part of Microsoft’s Copilot+ PC1 family—Windows 11 devices designed with built-in AI hardware.5

A Surface PC with AI includes the following:

  • Built-in AI chip (NPU)
    Surface Copilot+ PCs include an integrated Neural Processing Unit (NPU)—a dedicated, on-device AI processor which apps may use to run AI tasks directly. When apps use the NPU to offload and accelerate AI workloads, it can support advanced experiences, like image creation and real-time translation with accelerated speed and efficiency.5
  • New Copilot+ PC experiences in Windows
    On a Surface Copilot+ PC with AI, you can get Copilot+ PC experiences1 in Windows designed to take advantage of that NPU—for example:
  1. Find and re-discover things you’ve seen on your PC more easily with Recall.6
  2. Generate and enhance images in Microsoft Photos with Restyle Image,7 Image Creator,7 and Super Resolution.8
  3. Select text to choose quick, AI-powered actions like summarizing, creating a bulleted list, and rewriting—and much more with Click to Do.9


With a Surface PC, enjoy the same Windows desktop, apps, and Surface design you’re used to—but with AI built in.1

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What can I do with AI on a Surface Copilot+ PC?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

On a Surface Copilot+ PC,1 AI is built-in to help you work, create, and connect. With a Surface Copilot+ PC, you can do all this and more with AI:

  • Get writing and research help. Use Copilot on Windows10 on supported devices to draft or refine emails and documents, summarize content, ask questions, and get ideas in supported apps and services.
  • Learn more with ease. Use Copilot Vision11 on supported devices to receive insights and step-by-step guidance based on what Copilot sees on your screen.
  • Have conversations with AI. Use Copilot Voice12 on supported devices to talk with Copilot. Just tap or click the microphone on your phone, on the web, or on your laptop or PC to begin a voice chat with Copilot.
  • Edit and create images. On supported Copilot+ PCs,1 Microsoft Photos adds AI features like Restyle Image,7 Image Creator,7 and Super Resolution8 help you enhance or reimagine your photos.
  • Improve video calls and recordings. On supported Copilot+ PCs,1 Windows Studio Effects16 can adjust framing, blur your background, and enhance audio to help you look and sound your best on calls.
  • Do more, faster. On supported Copilot+ PCs,1 Recall6 helps you find and jump back into what you’ve already seen on your PC more easily, while Click to Do9 lets you take faster action to summarize, rewrite, and more.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Are AI PCs secure?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Microsoft designs Surface Copilot+ PCs1, with security built in. Protection starts in the hardware and continues through Windows 11 features and ongoing updates.5,13

Here’s some of what helps make Surface AI PCs secure:

  • Hardware-based protection. Surface Copilot+ AI PCs1 include the Microsoft Pluton security processor14 built into the CPU to help protect credentials, identities, and encryption keys.
  • Security built into Windows 11. Features such as Windows Hello,15 BitLocker, and Secure Boot help keep your information safer when enabled and kept up to date.
  • Automatic updates. Both Windows 11 and the Microsoft Pluton security processor14 receive regular security updates through Windows Update to help maintain protection over time.


Learn about even more ways Surface devices prioritize your security.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Which Surface PCs are good for gaming?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Surface PCs run Windows 11, which supports a wide range of PC games, gaming accessories, and cloud gaming services including Xbox Game Pass.

Note: The best Surface device for gaming depends on how you play—whether you’re playing PC games locally, using cloud gaming, or connecting accessories and external monitors for a larger setup.

Here are a few Surface models to consider based on performance capability and flexibility:

  • Surface Laptop (Copilot+ PC): Designed for performance and portability, Surface Laptop is well-suited for users who value a portable gaming laptop.
  • Surface Pro (Copilot+ PC): Surface Pro’s flexible design and all-day battery life17 make it a great 2-in-1 laptop for gaming at home or on the go.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I choose the best Surface Copilot+ PC for gaming and streaming?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Choosing a Surface Copilot+ PC for gaming and streaming depends on how you plan to use it—whether you game and stream separately, do both at the same time, or switch between different setups.

When comparing streaming and gaming PCs, consider:

  • How you plan to use gaming and streaming: Some players stream gameplay live, while others use streaming services to play games, watch content, or game casually. Understanding your usage helps narrow the right balance of performance and flexibility.
  • Support for accessories and monitors: Both gaming and streaming often involve external monitors, controllers, microphones, webcams, and other accessories. A PC that supports standard peripherals makes it easier to customize or expand your setup.
  • Connectivity and reliability: Stable connections and modern ports are important for both gaming and streaming, especially when using multiple accessories or external displays.
  • Flexibility in gaming options: Many players use a mix of local PC gaming and cloud gaming. Choosing a Windows PC that supports both gives you more ways to play and stream content.
  • Form factor and space: Whether you prefer a portable PC or a more stationary setup can influence what works best for gaming and streaming in your space.


For many users, the best PC for gaming and streaming is one that supports Windows gaming, works with common accessories and monitors, and adapts to different ways of playing and watching. Comparing devices side by side can help you find the option that fits your needs now and as they evolve. 

Surface Copilot+ PCs are designed for productivity and AI-powered experiences, but they can absolutely play games too. Many popular titles run well on Surface, especially with features like Auto Super Resolution that use on-device AI to enhance your gameplay. They're a great fit if you enjoy gaming as part of a well-rounded PC experience — catching up on your favorite games between classes or meetings, playing on the go with outstanding battery life, or streaming games via cloud gaming services like Xbox Cloud Gaming.

"}}]}, {"@type": "FAQPage", "@context": "http://schema.org", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I gift a digital game or Xbox Game Pass Ultimate online?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "It’s never been easier to send someone a near-instantaneous gift on Xbox or your PC. "What's the difference between Xbox Series X and Xbox Series S?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "
  • Both Xbox Series X|S consoles deliver next-generation capabilities powered by the Xbox Velocity Architecture, such as faster loading, the ability to seamlessly switch between multiple games with Quick Resume, richer and more dynamic worlds, and frame rates up to 120 FPS.
  • The primary difference between the two consoles is that the Xbox Series X displays games in native 4K, is available with a UHD Blu-ray Disc player or as a Disc-free console, and you can choose between 1TB SSD or 2TB SSD. Xbox Series S is designed for disc-free gaming at 1440p (with the ability to upscale to 4K) and you can choose between 512GB SSD or 1TB SSD. And, of course, they're priced differently. Both consoles have the option to expand available custom storage via the Storage Expansion Card. Compare Xbox consoles.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Which accessories are compatible with Xbox Series X|S?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Good news—a number of Xbox (including Xbox One) and third-party accessories such as controllers and headsets are compatible with Xbox Series X|S Learn more about compatible accessories or shop Xbox accessories here."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I connect my Xbox controller to PC?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Many PC games let you play with an Xbox Wireless Controller instead of a keyboard or mouse.

You can connect your controller to a Windows device by using a USB cable, the Xbox Wireless Adapter for Windows or Bluetooth. Some Windows devices also come with Xbox Wireless functionality built in so that you can connect a controller directly without an adaptor. Shop controllers here.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Which Xbox consoles play discs?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Xbox Series X (in carbon black) as well as Xbox Series X – 2 TB Galaxy Black Special Edition come equipped with a UHD Blu-ray Disc player."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I add warranty to a controller or Xbox console?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "
  • The easiest way to make sure you’re covered from the get-go is to add Microsoft Complete when you buy a new Xbox console, Xbox Wireless Controller or Xbox Elite Wireless Controller. You can also get Microsoft Complete by contacting one of our support service agents. Contact Microsoft Store Support
  • Already have Microsoft Complete? We can help with everything from registering your device to coverage status and expert technical support. Check Coverage Status
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": " What games can I play on an Xbox Series X|S console?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Xbox Series X|S consoles are compatible with thousands of games across four generations of Xbox. And, with Smart Delivery games, you can buy a digital game once and get the best version of that game for the console you’re playing on. To play a backwards compatible game, insert the disc or search your library and select the game you want to play. Learn more about compatible games and shop for games directly from your Xbox Series X console or online at Xbox.com."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is a certified refurbished console?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "The Microsoft Certified Refurbished program allows a pre-owned Xbox game console to be offered at a discounted price. These previously used game consoles undergo a complete refurbishment process before being offered for sale. Refurbished consoles are available exclusively at Microsoft Store."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": " How do I request a refund for a digital game?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

We understand that sometimes purchases of digital game products don’t go as planned. When that happens, we’ll help you request a refund. Refunds are typically issued for requests meeting the following criteria:

  • Requests are made within 14 days of the purchase date
  • Requests are for games in which you haven’t accumulated a significant amount of play time

Learn more here.

"target-global-mbox"}, {"@type": "FAQPage", "@context": "http://schema.org", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "Why are software updates necessary?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Microsoft is committed to providing its customers with software that has been tested for safety and security. Although no system is completely secure, we use processes, technology, and several specially focused teams to investigate, fix, and learn from security issues to help us meet this goal and to provide guidance to customers on how to help protect their PCs.

As part of the Microsoft software release process, all software released to the Download Center is scanned for malware before it is made available for public download. Additionally, after release, the software available from the Download Center is routinely scanned for malware. Microsoft recognizes that the threat environment is constantly changing and will continue to evolve over time, and we are committed to process improvements that will help protect our customers from malware threats

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I keep my software up to date?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft offers a range of online services to help you keep your computer up to date. Windows Update finds updates that you might not even be aware of and provides you with the simplest way to install updates that help prevent or fix problems, improve how your computer works, or enhance your computing experience. Visit Windows Update to learn more."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What can I find in the Microsoft Download Center, and how do I find what I’m looking for?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

The Microsoft Download Center has recently been revised to better serve you as a one-stop shop for products available for purchase, in addition to products and downloads available for free. For your convenience, items available for purchase are linked directly to Microsoft Store. Items available as free downloads are linked to details pages, where you can learn more about them and initiate downloads.

If you would like Microsoft to scan your computer and recommend updates, please see the preceding question. If you would like to search for specific downloads, start by using one of these methods.

  • To find products and downloads, in the navigation bar that appears across the center of the Download Center home page, click an icon.
  • To search more specifically for a product or download, on the menu bar displayed near the top of the page, click a menu name and then click a product or download.
  • To search by keyword, at the top of the page, in the Search Download Center search box, type the word or words you wish to search for.


On the Search results page, you may see the following:

  • An Editor's choice listing of products and downloads, which are recommendations based on the search term.
  • A Sort by box on the right side of the page. Click the down arrow, and select a display order for the search results.
  • A list of Filter options on the left side of the page. Click a Filter option to narrow the list of items displayed in the search results. The Filter option operates together with the original search term to narrow the scope of search results.


When you see the item you’re interested in, click the item link to find out more about downloading or purchasing it.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I find worldwide downloads?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Microsoft delivers downloads in more than 118 languages worldwide. The Download Center now combines all English downloads into a single English Download Center. We no longer offer separate downloads for U.S. English, U.K. English, Australian English, or Canadian English.

Select a Worldwide download from the English-language site

  1. On the download details page for a particular item, under Quick details, you will see a Change language box. Select the desired language from the drop-down list

Note: The Change Language box appears only when a worldwide version of the download is available. If the box does not appear, the download has not yet been made available in languages other than English. If you see the box, but a specific language is not listed, the download is not yet available in that language.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Which other Microsoft websites offer downloads?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

In this section, you will find links to other Microsoft websites that offer downloads. Note that almost all of the downloads available from these websites can also be found in the Microsoft Download Center; however, the other Microsoft sites may offer services and information that you may not find in the Download Center.

If you are looking for downloads for the Windows operating system or for Microsoft Office, try using Windows Update, which helps you keep your computer up to date without requiring you to visit the Download Center.

  • DirectX downloads

    Get the latest version of Microsoft DirectX, and find other important DirectX updates for your version of Windows.

  • Service Packs for Microsoft Products

    Download service packs containing updates for system reliability, program compatibility, and security. See which service packs are currently available from Microsoft Help and Support.

  • Windows Media Download Center

    Amplify your play with Microsoft Windows Media Player, encoders, codecs, and other related downloads.

  • Azure downloads

    Get SDKs and tools for managing and interacting with Azure services

  • Windows developer resources

    Get downloads and tools for Windows development

  • Code samples

    Get started with Microsoft developer tools and technologies. Explore our samples and discover the things you can build.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What should I do if I can’t find what I am looking for?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "If you cannot find a specific download, it may be available from a company other than Microsoft. Popular examples include Adobe Reader, Macromedia Shockwave and Flash players, and Java software. For current links to these downloads on their respective websites, use Bing to search the web for the download you are looking for. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What information will I find on download pages?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

When you click a download item in the Download Center, you will be directed to the download details page for that download. Most of these pages follow a standard format and include most of the sections described here.

  • Quick details

    The Quick details section contains helpful summary information, including the name and size of the download file and its date of publication.

  • Change language

    In some cases, you'll see a Change language box, from which you can select a language other than English. Note that some downloads are not available in all languages. In addition, not all non-English downloads are accompanied by localized details pages. As a result, you may find English descriptions and instructions on pages for non-English downloads.

  • Download button or link

    For each file listed, you will see a Download button or link in the Quick details section. Click this button or link to begin the download.

  • Overview

    The Overview section includes a brief explanation of the issues addressed by the download and the benefits it provides.

  • System requirements

    Always check the System requirements section to make sure your computer is compatible with and has the space required to install and run the download. This section may also list required or recommended application software.

  • Instructions

    Review the Instructions section before clicking the Download button, so you are prepared for any steps you may need to follow.

  • What others are downloading

    This lists other items downloaded by people who also downloaded the current item. Information about these related downloads can be very helpful.

  • Additional information

    This provides further information that is either directly related to the current download or is meant to help keep your system running securely, such as security update information.

  • Related resources

    These links take you to how-to information, newsgroups, special offers, and other resources pertinent to the download you have selected.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What should I do if I cannot complete a download?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "If you cannot complete a download, you may need to clear the cache in your browser.  Here are Instructions for how to clear the cache in Microsoft Edge."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I install downloaded software?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Before you can use any software that you download, you must install it. For example, if you download a security update but do not install it, the update will not provide any protection for your computer. Follow these steps to save and install a download:

  1. On the download details page, click the Download button.
  2. A dialogue box will open. Click the Save button.
  3. You will be prompted to select a location for the download on your computer. Select an appropriate location, and click the Save button.
  4. When the file has finished downloading, you will see a second dialog box. If you want to install the file immediately, click the Open button. This launches an installation wizard.
  5. If you do not want to immediately install the file, click the Close button. When you are ready to install the file, double-click the file to launch the installation wizard.

Note: When you save a download file, make sure to save it to a location that you will remember when you are ready to install the download. We suggest creating a special folder on your desktop to which you always save downloads. Follow these steps to create such a downloads folder:

  1. Right-click an unused area of your desktop.
  2. Point to New, and then click Folder.
  3. Give the folder a name, such as My Downloads.
"}}]}, {"cookieMaxAgeSeconds": 1800, "isInScope": false, "targetModes": ["targetPageParamsAll", "getOffer"], "targetMboxName": "target-global-mbox"}, {"@type": "FAQPage", "@context": "http://schema.org", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "What can I purchase with a Microsoft gift card?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "With a Microsoft gift card, you can buy apps, games, movies, and more from the Microsoft Store, on Windows PCs, and on Xbox. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is Surface Laptop good for students?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. At your direction, it can search your files, understand what’s on your screen with Copilot Vision, and help you across apps and documents.

Copilot Vision on Windows is available in over 170 markets worldwide through the Copilot on Windows app. Usage limits for Copilot Vision will vary over time."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I start using Copilot on Windows?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "You can launch Copilot on Windows by clicking the Copilot icon on your taskbar. Learn more about Copilot on Windows, including Voice and Vision, at Copilot on Windows.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Do I have to download anything to use Windows 11 AI-powered tools?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Windows 11 comes with several built-in AI-powered features such as the Snipping Tool, voice access, and smart recommendations in File Explorer and the Start menu. Additionally, it can automate tasks to speed up your workflow."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What AI features are in Clipchamp?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Clipchamp makes creating and editing videos simple and powerful with AI-driven tools:
  • AI text to speech: generate a flawless AI voiceover in global languages to make videos with great reach.
  • AI video editor: unleash your inner director with auto compose to quickly generate engaging short videos.
  • AI silence remover: find and erase unwanted silences and pauses automatically in any media.
  • AI background noise remover: clean up, boost clarity, and remove background noises without losing audio quality.
  • AI subtitles: create accessible and easy-to-follow videos with subtitles in any language.
  • AI background removal: remove the background of any image and replace with stock video, images, solid colors, and more.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What AI features are in Paint?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Paint now comes with new AI-powered tools to help you edit images like a pro and create art like a visionary.   • Image Creator is your AI-powered partner, ready to inspire your next creation. Give it a prompt, pick a style, and see where it takes you.
  • Background removal uses AI to help you isolate a subject and remove the background with just one click.

To use Image Creator, you need to sign in with your Microsoft account. Signing in with your Microsoft account also allows you to access your credits, which are needed to generate images. Learn more.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How does AI enhance security in Windows 11?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "AI enhances security in Windows 11 through a variety of features designed to protect against evolving cyber threats and to simplify security for users and IT professionals. AI is used in the Smart App Control* in Windows 11 to predict which apps are safe to download and run while blocking unknown apps often associated with new malware. This proactive approach is designed to simplify security and make Windows more secure out of the box.

*Only available on the latest version of Windows 11. In order to keep you as safe as possible, you must start with a fresh system. PCs that have been running previous versions of Windows must do a full reset of the Windows 11 operating system for the feature to work. Only available in North America and Europe."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I stay up to date on the latest AI features in Windows?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Windows 11 helps ensure your device remains secure and current by automatically downloading and installing updates including the latest AI features. Furthermore, users have the option to update the pre-installed apps to their latest versions via Microsoft Store, independent of the Windows 11 OS updates. Microsoft Store will automatically update apps in the background. If your PC is managed by an organization, it may be that they disable updates. Contact your administrator to find out how your device These include laptops, desktops, 2-in-1s, all-in-ones, gaming PCs, AI PCs, and more. In simple terms, Windows is the software that helps you do everyday things on your computer, like browse the web, open apps, manage files, stream content, create documents, and personalize your experience. New Windows PCs run Windows 11, the latest version of the OS. What makes a Windows computer appealing to many people is the range of choices. Instead of just one device style, Windows is available across many brands, screen sizes, designs, and performance levels. That means you can find a Windows PC for everyday use, school, work, gaming, or creative projects—whatever matters most to you."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the newest Windows 11 computer?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "There’s no single newest Windows 11 computer, because Windows 11 is available on many new PCs from a variety of manufacturers. That means the newest Windows 11 computer can look very different depending on the brand you prefer and whether you want something ultra-portable, performance-focused, or built for AI-powered experiences."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What devices can run Windows 11?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Windows 11 runs on many types of PCs, including laptops, desktops, 2-in-1s, all-in-ones, gaming PCs, and AI-powered Copilot+ PCs. Microsoft also offers Windows 11 PCs built for a variety of uses, from everyday browsing and schoolwork to gaming, creative projects, and multitasking."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the best PC for graphic design?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "The best PC for graphic design combines strong performance with a high-quality display to support visual work. Eligible college and university students in the United States who purchase a new, qualified Windows PC may redeem 1 year of Microsoft 365 Premium, 1 year of Xbox Game Pass Ultimate, and an Xbox Design Lab Wireless Controller.   • Offer applies to new Microsoft 365 Premium subscribers and new Xbox Game Pass subscribers only.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I redeem the offer?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Redemption must be completed by 11:59pm Pacific Time on July 31, 2026. To initiate redemption of the offer subscriptions and Xbox Wireless Controller, customers must verify their student eligibility using their .edu college email address. Customers who purchased an eligible device will be notified in two ways to check for their student eligibility:

  • On their new PC, students will see an on-screen offer notification after device setup to verify their student status.
  • An email with offer details will be sent to the Microsoft account email address registered with the eligible device reminding them to verify their college student status.

Step 1 – Student verification:

  • After clicking “Verify now” from the Windows screen or email, customers will be directed to the Microsoft Academic Verification page. Please sign in with the Microsoft account email registered with the eligible Windows PC, fill out the form, and provide their .edu college email address for verification.

Step 2 – Claim the offer:

  • After student verification is successfully completed, students will receive an email to claim their Xbox Game Pass Ultimate subscription, Microsoft 365 Premium subscription, and the unique promo code to redeem the controller. If you view the email on your mobile phone, please note that you can only claim your Xbox Game Pass Ultimate subscription on your eligible PC.
  • A valid payment method is required to redeem the subscriptions, and, after the 1-year promotional period, you will be charged the applicable monthly subscription fee. You will be notified of any price changes and may cancel at any time by visiting account.microsoft.com.
  • If the Xbox Game Pass Ultimate subscription is activated prior to student verification, you must complete that verification within 5 days of such activation or the Xbox Game Pass Ultimate subscription will be deactivated.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Who are considered eligible students?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What devices are eligible for this offer?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What happens after the free subscriptions end? Do they auto-renew?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Eligible college and university students in the United States who purchase a new, qualified Windows PC may redeem 1 year of Microsoft 365 Premium, 1 year of XBOX Game Pass Ultimate, and an XBOX Design Lab Wireless Controller. Basic color selection of the controller is included; other options such as limited-edition designs; different finishing, patterns, rubberized grips, metallic colors; and engraving are available for an additional cost.   • Offer applies to new Microsoft 365 Premium subscribers and new XBOX Game Pass subscribers only.
  • Verification of higher education student status from an accredited university or college in the U.S. with a valid .edu email address is required.
  • Offer available in U.S. only.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I redeem the offer?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Redemption must be completed by 11:59pm Pacific Time on July 31, 2026. To initiate redemption of the offer subscriptions and XBOX Wireless Controller, customers must verify their student eligibility using their .edu college email address. Customers who purchased an eligible device will be notified in two ways to check for their student eligibility:

  • On their new PC, students will see an on-screen offer notification after device setup to check for qualification.
  • An email with offer details will be sent to the Microsoft account email address registered with the eligible device reminding them to verify their college student status.

Step 1 – Student verification:

  • After clicking “Verify now” from the Windows screen or email, customers will be directed to the Microsoft Academic Verification page. Please sign in with the Microsoft account email registered with the eligible Windows PC, fill out the form, and provide their .edu college email address for verification.

Step 2 – Claim the offer:

  • After student verification is successfully completed, students will receive an email to claim their XBOX Game Pass Ultimate subscription, Microsoft 365 Premium subscription, and the unique promo code to redeem the controller. If you view the email on your mobile phone, please note that you can only claim your XBOX Game Pass Ultimate subscription on your eligible PC.
  • A valid payment method is required to redeem the subscriptions, and, after the 1-year promotional period, you will be charged the applicable monthly subscription fee. You will be notified of any price changes and may cancel at any time by visiting account.microsoft.com.
  • If the XBOX Game Pass Ultimate subscription is activated prior to student verification, you must complete that verification within 5 days of such activation or the XBOX Game Pass Ultimate subscription will be deactivated.

Choosing the right computer depends on how—and where—you plan to use it. Here are a few key factors to consider:

  • Your primary use case: Will you mostly be working, creating, streaming, or multitasking? Your activities help determine the processing power, storage, and memory you'll need.
  • Where you’ll use it: If you’re on the go, look for lightweight options like Surface Laptops.6
  • Device type that fits your style: Consider whether you want a classic laptop, a 2-in-1 for flexibility, or an all-in-one desktop for a larger workspace.
  • AI-ready features: Look for a computer with next-level AI experiences, like Copilot+ PCs.**
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Do Surface PCs come with AI capabilities?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "New Surface PCs running Windows 11 come with Copilot on Windows pre-installed."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Do Surface PCs come with a warranty?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. New Surface computers come with a 1-year limited warranty in the US, but the length varies by market."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do Surface PCs enhance productivity?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Our touchscreen laptops, 2-in-1s, and all-in-one PCs are designed to help you work, play, and create in your own unique way."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Surface Laptop vs. Surface Pro: What is the difference?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

The key difference between Surface Laptop and Surface Pro is form factor: Surface Pro is a 2-in-1 device that functions as both a tablet and laptop, while Surface Laptop is a traditional laptop.

  • Surface Pro: A lightweight,6,17 versatile 2-in-1 device that works as both a tablet and laptop. Pair it with accessories including Surface Slim Pen and a detachable keyboard.
  • Surface Laptop: A traditional laptop with a built-in keyboard, slim design,17 and long battery life2—great if you want a classic laptop experience with touchscreen functionality.


Learn more about the differences between the Surface Pro and Surface Laptop.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Laptop vs. Tablet vs. 2-in-1 PC: What is the difference?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

The difference between a laptop, tablet, and 2-in-1 PC comes down to how each device is built and how you plan to use it.

  • Laptop: A portable computer with a built-in keyboard, generally designed for productivity and everyday work.
  • Tablet: A lightweight touchscreen device built for portability, entertainment, and simple tasks.

{"@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "FAQPage", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "Do I need a Microsoft account for Windows 11?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Yes. A Microsoft account is required to set up a device with Windows 11 Home or Windows 11 Pro. Your Microsoft account comes with 5 GB OneDrive cloud storage and access to free web and mobile versions of Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook. If you don’t have a Microsoft account, it’s easy to sign up using your existing or new email address here.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is Microsoft OneDrive?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

OneDrive is a Microsoft cloud storage service that lets you store your personal files in one place, share them with others, and access them from any device connected to the internet. Learn more about OneDrive.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I add a Microsoft account to OneDrive?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Learn how to add accounts in OneDrive.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How much cloud storage is available on my Microsoft account?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

A Microsoft account includes 5 GB of cloud storage with OneDrive. If you need more space for your files, you can upgrade to a qualifying Microsoft 365 subscription, which offers additional storage and premium features. See available options.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I transfer my files using an external storage device?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Yes, you can bring your files with you using an external storage device such as a USB drive, SD card, or external hard drive. Learn how.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I use Microsoft Office on a new computer?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Yes. You can install or activate compatible versions of Office on your new computer using your product key.

Activate Office on a new PC >

How to install Office >

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I transfer older programs and applications to a new Windows computer?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Windows Backup helps you move files, settings, and a list of your apps when setting up a new Windows 11 PC. It works with PCs running Windows 10 or later and restores on Windows 11, version 22H2 or higher.

Keep in mind that apps themselves aren’t automatically transferred or installed. When you open an app for the first time on your new PC, some can be reinstalled directly from the Microsoft Store. Others may need to be reinstalled manually from the original provider. Geographic restrictions may apply. See WOA for Arm-compatible apps and Windows Backup for details.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I trade in my older Windows 10 laptop or PC for an upgrade?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Microsoft Store offers a Trade-in Program powered by Teladvance in select countries. You may be eligible for cash back if your device meets the criteria for offers currently available in your country. Just answer a few questions about your device’s brand, model, and condition to see if it qualifies. Get started by checking your device’s estimated trade-in value. Available trade-in offers and eligibility requirements apply and may vary over time.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the Windows 10 Extended Security Updates (ESU) program?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Microsoft announced last October the Extended Security Updates (ESU) consumer program that gives the option to receive critical and important security updates for eligible Windows 10 PCs enrolled in the program through October 13, 2026. The program provides you with more time to stay protected on your current Windows 10 device before moving to a new Surface PC.

For additional information on Consumer ESU options, please visit ESU Settings or the ESU support page.

For additional information on Commercial ESU program, please visit the Microsoft Learn page.

"}}]}, {"@type": "FAQPage", "@context": "http://schema.org", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I choose the right new computer?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Choosing the right computer depends on how—and where—you plan to use it. Here are a few key factors to consider:

  • Your primary use case: Will you mostly be working, creating, streaming, or multitasking? Your activities help determine the processing power, storage, and memory you'll need.
  • Where you’ll use it: If you’re on the go, look for lightweight options like Surface Laptops.6
  • Device type that fits your style: Consider whether you want a classic laptop, a 2-in-1 for flexibility, or an all-in-one desktop for a larger workspace.
  • AI-ready features: Look for a computer with next-level AI experiences, like Copilot+ PCs.**
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Do Surface PCs come with AI capabilities?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "New Surface PCs running Windows 11 come with Copilot on Windows pre-installed."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Do Surface PCs come with a warranty?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. New Surface computers come with a 1-year limited warranty in the US, but the length varies by market."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do Surface PCs enhance productivity?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Our touchscreen laptops, 2-in-1s, and all-in-one PCs are designed to help you work, play, and create in your own unique way."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Surface Laptop vs. Surface Pro: What is the difference?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

The key difference between Surface Laptop and Surface Pro is form factor: Surface Pro is a 2-in-1 device that functions as both a tablet and laptop, while Surface Laptop is a traditional laptop.

  • Surface Pro: A lightweight,6,17 versatile 2-in-1 device that works as both a tablet and laptop. Pair it with accessories including Surface Slim Pen and a detachable keyboard.
  • Surface Laptop: A traditional laptop with a built-in keyboard, slim design,17 and long battery life2—great if you want a classic laptop experience with touchscreen functionality.


Learn more about the differences between the Surface Pro and Surface Laptop.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Laptop vs. Tablet vs. 2-in-1 PC: What is the difference?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

The difference between a laptop, tablet, and 2-in-1 PC comes down to how each device is built and how you plan to use it.

  • Laptop: A portable computer with a built-in keyboard, generally designed for productivity and everyday work.
  • Tablet: A lightweight touchscreen device built for portability, entertainment, and simple tasks.
  • 2-in-1 PC: A versatile device that can function as both a laptop and tablet—often featuring a detachable or foldable keyboard—so you can switch between keyboard typing and touch-only modes as needed.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Does Microsoft Surface offer business laptops and computers?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Yes. Microsoft offers a full lineup of Surface PCs designed for business, including models like Surface Pro 10 for Business or Surface Laptop 6 for Business. These business devices include:

  • Windows 11 Pro
  • Chip-to-cloud security features
  • Long battery life2
  • Copilot+ PC AI experiences**
  • Management tools such as Windows Autopilot32


Learn more about Surface for Business Laptops and Computers.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Business laptop vs. consumer laptop: What is the difference?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

When you compare business laptops vs. consumer laptops in the Surface lineup, the biggest differences usually come down to software, level of security, and how the devices are meant to be used.31

  • Consumer laptops: Surface Pro or Surface Laptop consumer laptops come with Windows 11 and security features including a Microsoft Pluton34 security chip that helps protect credentials, user identities, encryption keys, and personal data. The Windows Hello35,36 uses face authentication with Enhanced Sign-in security, all for the purpose of everyday computing.
  • Business laptops: Surface Pro for Business or Surface Laptop for Business—comes with Windows 11 Pro, additional chip-to-cloud security including Bitlocker,37 remote firmware management,33 and other features designed for businesses.
"}}]}, {"@type": "FAQPage", "@context": "http://schema.org", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "How does Windows Hello keep my info private?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Windows Hello enables a passwordless sign-in. It binds your credentials to your device. Just like how your debit card has a PIN, Windows Hello authentication validates your credentials based on either a specific PIN for your device or a biometrics match (a scan of your fingerprint or facial recognition). Because this data never leaves your PC and is never collected by our servers, these credentials cannot be used by anyone who does not have physical access to your device—protecting you from typical replay, phishing, spoofing, and other network attacks, including password leaks!"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What should I do when Windows Hello doesn't recognize me, or I get an error message when I try to sign in? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

If your face or fingerprint didn’t scan properly, or if you’re using the wrong finger to sign in, you’ll get an error message when you try to sign in through Windows Hello. If that happens, sign in with a PIN or password instead, and then set up Windows Hello again.

If you get an error message that says ‘Couldn't recognize you. Please sign in with your PIN.’ when you try to sign in with Windows Hello, follow the steps below to improve recognition. This error message can occur if you wear glasses and didn't set up face recognition to recognize you both with and without your glasses. This might also occur if the device has been moved and the lighting conditions are dramatically different in the new location.

To improve recognition, select Start  > Settings   > Accounts > Sign-in options > Facial recognition (Windows Hello), and then select Improve recognition.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Why should I keep my PC locked?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Keeping your PC locked prevents unauthorized users from accessing your files and sensitive information. Locking your device also prevents others from tampering with your files or viewing emails or social media messages. You should lock your device anytime you leave it unattended."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I lock my PC screen?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

An easy way to lock your PC is with Dynamic lock. Dynamic lock uses Bluetooth to pair with your phone, so when you leave your device, it knows you’re no longer near it. Once you return to your device, sign back in with Windows Hello.

To enable Dynamic lock, Select Start  > Settings   > Accounts > Sign-in options, and then select Allow Windows to automatically lock your device when you’re away. 

"}}]}, {"@type": "FAQPage", "@context": "http://schema.org", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "What is Microsoft Complete?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft Complete is an extended protection plan available for select Microsoft devices (such as eligible Surface devices and Xbox consoles, depending on your market). Microsoft Complete generally includes protection for mechanical breakdowns and may include accidental damage from handling (for example, drops or spills), subject to limits, exclusions, and any applicable service fees and claim allowances in the plan terms."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How much does Microsoft Complete cost?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Pricing varies by device, coverage term, and country/region. You’ll see the price for your eligible device during purchase (or when adding Microsoft Complete, where available)."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How is Microsoft Complete different from the standard Microsoft warranty?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "The standard Microsoft limited warranty generally covers certain defects in materials and workmanship for a limited period. If offered for your device and region, Microsoft Complete may include accidental damage coverage—see the plan terms for details."}}]}, {"@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "FAQPage", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "What are the minimum system requirements for Windows 11?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "These are the minimum system requirements for installing Windows 11 on a PC:
  • A 1 GHz or faster processor with 2 cores or more
  • 4 GB of RAM
  • 64 GB of storage or more
  • UEFI firmware with Secure Boot capability
  • TPM version 2.0
  • DirectX 12 compatible graphics card
  • Display larger than 9 inches diagonally with HD resolution (720p)
  • Windows 11 Pro for personal use and Windows 11 Home require internet connectivity and a Microsoft account during initial device setup
  • Internet access is required to perform for updates and take advantage of some features
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is a central processing unit (CPU) or processor?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "The central processing unit (CPU), often called the processor, is the brain of your computer. It interprets instructions and tells every component what to do, from running apps to managing data. A faster CPU means your PC can handle more tasks at once, making everything feel quicker and more responsive. Watch now to learn more about your computer’s CPU."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is RAM and why is it important?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "RAM is like your computer's quick-access memory, keeping the data you need right now at your fingertips. With more RAM, your PC can run faster and handle multiple tasks more smoothly, making everything feel more responsive, allowing you to do more at the same time. Watch now to learn more about your computer’s RAM."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is an NPU and why is it important?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "An NPU, or Neural Processing Unit, accelerates AI features like image generation and live captions on Windows devices to help you work faster and be more creative. Watch now to learn more about NPUs.

An NPU is important because it improves how AI works on your device by:

  • Allowing certain AI features to keep working even when you’re offline, since the NPU can run AI directly on your PC.
  • Using less power than the CPU or GPU for AI tasks, which helps features run smoothly without noticeably affecting battery life or heat.
  • Processing more data on your device instead of in the cloud, which can help reduce how often personal information needs to leave your PC.
  • Supporting real-time AI features that can run in the background without slowing down everyday use.
  • Enabling your PC to add new AI capabilities over time through software updates, thanks to dedicated on-device AI hardware.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is United Extensible Firmware Interface (UEFI)?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "When the device starts, the firmware interface controls the booting process of the PC and then passes control to Windows or another operating system. UEFI is a replacement for the older BIOS firmware interface and the Extensible Firmware Interface (EFI) 1.10 specifications. More than 140 leading technology companies participate in the Unified EFI Forum, including AMD, AMI, Apple, Dell, HP, IBM, Insyde, Intel, Lenovo, Microsoft, and Phoenix Technologies."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is TPM (Trusted Platform Module)?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "The Trusted Platform Module (TPM) technology is designed to provide hardware-based, security-related functions. A TPM chip is a secure crypto-processor that is designed to carry out cryptographic operations. The chip includes multiple physical security mechanisms to make it tamper-resistant, and malicious software is unable to tamper with the security functions of the TPM. Some of the advantages of using TPM technology are to:
  • Generate, store, and limit the use of cryptographic keys.
  • Use it for device authentication by using the TPM's unique RSA key, which is burned into the chip.
  • Help ensure platform integrity by taking and storing security measurements of the boot process.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is Secure Boot?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Secure Boot is a security feature that ensures your PC only starts with trusted software. It helps protect your system from malware and unauthorized changes during the startup process and keeps your device more secure from the moment you power it on."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is my computer compatible with Windows 11?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "You can install the PC Health Check app for free to determine if your PC supports Windows 11. Learn more about how to use the PC Health Check app to check for Windows 11 compatibility."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What are Copilot+ PCs?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Copilot+ PCs are a class of Windows 11 AI PCs that are powered by a turbocharged neural processing unit (NPU)—a specialized computer chip for AI-intensive processes like real-time translations and image generation— that can perform more than 40 trillion operations per second (TOPS).

In addition to the minimum system requirements for Windows 11, Copilot+ PCs must include the following:

  • Processor: A compatible processor or System on a Chip (SoC) with an NPU capable of performing 40+ TOPS. This currently includes:
    • AMD Ryzen™ AI 300 and 400 series
    • Intel® Core™ Ultra 200V and 300V series
    • Snapdragon® X series
  • RAM: 16 GB DDR5/LPDDR5
  • Storage: 256 GB SSD/UFS

Specific features, plus apps and hardware that you add to your Copilot+ PC may have additional hardware, software, or other requirements, and these requirements may change over time.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is my PC compatible with Microsoft Copilot?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "If your PC runs Windows 11 it’s already compatible with Copilot. You can access Copilot’s built-in AI features like assistance for writing, summarizing content, and more.

To unlock Copilot+ PC experiences on your device, like improved Windows search and agent in Settings, you’ll need a Copilot+ PC.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What hardware is needed for AI experiences?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Windows 11 supports cloud-based AI experiences through apps like Microsoft Copilot and Copilot Mode in Microsoft Edge. To stay updated on seasonal and ongoing offers, you can visit the Microsoft Store deals page, sign up for email alerts, or follow Microsoft on social media for announcements about upcoming promotions."}}]}, {"@type": "FAQPage", "@context": "http://schema.org", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "What’s the difference between Office 2024 (one-time purchase) and Microsoft 365 (subscription)?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Office 2024 is sold as a one-time purchase, which means you pay a single, up-front cost to get Office apps for one computer. One-time purchases are available for both PCs and Macs. However, there are no upgrade options, which means if you plan to upgrade to the next major release, you'll have to buy it at full price.

Microsoft 365 Personal, Microsoft 365 Family, and Microsoft 365 Premium are subscriptions that include powerful productivity apps and creativity tools with AI-powered features. In addition to desktop versions of popular Microsoft 365 apps like Word, PowerPoint, Excel, and Outlook with Microsoft Copilot, you also get spacious cloud storage, advanced security, and cloud-connected features that let you collaborate on files in real time. With a subscription, you'll always have the latest features, fixes, and security updates along with ongoing tech support at no extra cost. You can choose to pay for your subscription on a monthly or yearly basis, and use your apps on multiple PCs, Macs, tablets, and phones. Additionally, the Microsoft 365 Family plan lets you share your subscription with up to five more people. Everyone gets their own apps and storage. And with Microsoft 365 Premium, you also get extensive usage on advanced AI experiences and access to Copilot features currently exclusive to Premium subscribers. (AI features only available to subscription owner and cannot be shared; AI usage limits apply; minimum age limits may apply to subscription activation and use of AI features. Learn more.)

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I know if my PC or Mac can run Microsoft 365?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft 365 is compatible with PC, Mac, Android, and iOS. See system requirements for compatible versions of your devices, and for other feature requirements."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Will Microsoft 365 be identical on a PC and on a Mac?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No. Microsoft 365’s applications are tailored for each platform and each operating system. The applications available for Mac users and the specific features included may be different from those available for PC users. With Microsoft 365, you can be flexible. With your account, you are not limited to exclusively Mac or exclusively PC, so you can transition across devices."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Do I keep control of my documents with a Microsoft 365 subscription?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. Documents that you have created belong fully to you. You can choose to store them online on OneDrive or locally on your PC or Mac."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is internet access required for Microsoft 365?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Internet access is required to install and activate all the latest releases of apps and services included in all Microsoft 365 subscription plans. Note that if you are an existing subscriber, you do not need to reinstall or purchase another subscription.

For Microsoft 365 plans, internet access is also needed to manage your subscription account, for example to install Office apps on other PCs or to change billing options. Internet access is also required to access documents stored on OneDrive, unless you install the OneDrive desktop app.

You should also connect to the internet regularly to keep your version of Microsoft 365 up to date and to benefit from automatic upgrades. If you do not connect to the internet at least every 31 days, your apps will go into reduced functionality mode, which means that you can view or print your documents but cannot edit the documents or create new ones. To reactivate your apps, simply reconnect to the internet.

You do not need to be connected to the internet to use the Office apps, such as Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, because the apps are fully installed on your computer.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is a Microsoft account and why do I need it for Microsoft 365?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Your Microsoft account is the combination of an email address and password that you use to sign in to services like OneDrive, Xbox LIVE, and Outlook.com. If you use any of these services, you already have a Microsoft account that you can use or you can create a new account. Learn more about a Microsoft account.

As part of signing up for a trial or purchasing Microsoft 365, you will be prompted to sign in with a Microsoft account. You must be signed in with this account to install and manage your Microsoft 365 subscription, or to use some subscription benefits, including cloud storage.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How many people can use a Microsoft 365 subscription?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "You can share Microsoft 365 Family and Microsoft 365 Premium with five other people, for a total of six users. Microsoft 365 Personal can be used by one person."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I share my subscription benefits with members of my family?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

If you have an active Microsoft 365 Family or Microsoft 365 Premium subscription, you can share it with up to five other people. Each person you share your subscription with can install Microsoft 365 on all their devices and sign in to five devices at the same time.

To add someone to your subscription, sign in to your Microsoft account and follow the on-screen instructions to add a user. Each person you add will receive an email with the steps they need to follow. Once they have accepted and completed the steps, their information, including the installs they are using, will appear on their My account page. You can stop sharing your subscription with someone or remove a device they are using by logging into your Microsoft account.

Microsoft Defender is a new cross-device app that helps people and families stay safer online. Microsoft Defender adds new features and a simplified, user interface. Microsoft Defender also brings valuable device protection to iOS, Android, Windows, and Mac, with malware protection, web protection, real-time security notifications, and security tips. Microsoft Defender is available in the Apple, Google, and Microsoft app stores and requires a Microsoft 365 Personal or Family subscription to use.

Windows Security, formerly known as Windows Defender Security Center, is built-in security on Windows PCs to protect your device and data. Windows Security is pre-installed and automatically enabled. Windows Security includes Microsoft Defender Antivirus software that protects your Windows device and data against viruses, ransomware, trojans, and other malware unless non-Microsoft Antivirus software is active.

"http://schema.org", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the Microsoft Store price protection policy?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

For purchases made outside the window of October 1, 2025 – December 2, 2025: Our price protection policy offers a one-time price adjustment on physical products purchased from Microsoft Store if our price—or the price at an eligible retailer—drops below the price you paid within 60 days of your delivery date. The physical product must be identical to the one you purchased (i.e., the exact make, model, color, and configuration), in stock at Microsoft Store (and new and in stock at the eligible retailer, if you’re requesting an eligible-retailer price adjustment), and listed at the reduced price at the time you contact us. Some exclusions apply. 

For purchases made between October 1, 2025 – December 2, 2025: Holiday extended price protection for Microsoft Store price adjustment valid on purchases of qualifying physical consumer products purchased from Microsoft Store online and Microsoft Experience centers in select markets between October 1, 2025 – December 2, 2025. Holiday purchase price adjustment may be requested until January 31, 2026. The physical product must be identical to the one you purchased (i.e., the exact make, model, color, and configuration), in stock at Microsoft Store, and listed at the reduced price at the time you contact us. Some exclusions apply.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I know if I’m eligible for a price adjustment on my purchase? How do I get a price adjustment?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

For purchases made outside the window of October 1, 2025 – December 2, 2025: You’ll need to contact Microsoft Store Sales and Support and request the price adjustment within 60 days of your delivery date. To be eligible for the price adjustment, the physical product must be identical to the one you purchased (i.e., the exact make, model, color, and configuration), in stock at Microsoft Store (and new and in stock at the eligible retailer, if you’re requesting an eligible-retailer price adjustment), and listed at the reduced price at the time you contact us. If you’re requesting an eligible-retailer price adjustment, a Microsoft Store associate must also be able to verify the advertised price of the eligible retailer. Some exclusions apply. 

For purchases made between October 1, 2025 – December 2, 2025: You’ll need to contact Microsoft Store Sales and Support and request the Microsoft Store price adjustment by January 31, 2026. To be eligible for the price adjustment, the physical product must be identical to the one you purchased (i.e., the exact make, model, color, and configuration), in stock at Microsoft Store, and listed at the reduced price at the time you contact us. Some exclusions apply.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How and when will I receive my refunded price adjustment?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "If your product is eligible for a price adjustment, we’ll credit the difference back to the original payment method used to purchase the item. Refunds are generally processed within 3-5 business days. Price adjustments for Surface for Business devices can be made via credit card for purchases made on Microsoft.com website only. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "My product is within the required time frame, but it’s no longer listed in stock at Microsoft Store. Is it eligible?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No, only products in stock at Microsoft Store at the time you contact Microsoft Store Sales and Support are eligible for a price adjustment."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I get both a one-time Microsoft Store price adjustment and a one-time eligible-retailer price adjustment on the same item?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No, there is a maximum of one price adjustment per purchased item."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Which products are eligible for a price adjustment?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Most physical consumer products and Surface for Business devices and accessories purchased from Microsoft Store are eligible for a Microsoft Store price adjustment. Most new physical consumer products purchased from Microsoft Store are eligible for an eligible-retailer price adjustment. Please note that ROG Xbox Ally, ROG Xbox Ally X, Surface for Business devices and accessories, and refurbished products are excluded from eligible-retailer price adjustments. Surface Hub, HoloLens, and Windows DevKit, as well as all digital products, are excluded from both price adjustments."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Am I eligible for a price adjustment if I purchased a bundle at Microsoft Store?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Bundles do not qualify for eligible-retailer price adjustments.
