", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes, only products purchased from Microsoft Store are eligible for a Microsoft Store price adjustment."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Which retailers are eligible for an eligible-retailer price adjustment?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "For Surface products, eligible retailers are limited to Best Buy and Amazon. For Xbox products, eligible retailers are limited to Best Buy, Amazon, Target, GameStop, and Walmart. The physical product must be fulfilled directly by the eligible retailer (i.e., not sold by a third-party or private seller on a “marketplace” platform). For purchases made between October 1, 2025 – December 2, 2025: Holiday extended price protection valid on refurbished devices purchased from Microsoft Store online and Microsoft Experience centers in select markets between October 1, 2025 – December 2, 2025. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Do commercial devices and accessories qualify for an eligible-retailer price adjustment?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No, Surface for Business devices and accessories, are not eligible for an eligible-retailer price adjustment. ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Intel and Qualcomm Snapdragon® processors differ mainly in architecture and efficiency. Intel Core Ultra processors are widely used in traditional Windows PCs and are known for broad software compatibility and performance. Qualcomm Snapdragon® processors use ARM technology, prioritizing power efficiency and battery life. Both run Windows, but they can deliver different performance, battery, and feature experiences depending on the device. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the difference between LCD and OLED? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "LCD (Liquid Crystal Display) screens use a backlight to display images with consistent brightness and sharp resolution, making them well suited for everyday tasks like email, web browsing, document work, and video calls. OLED (Organic Light Emitting Diode) screens light each pixel individually to display images with deeper blacks, higher contrast, and support for HDR, making them a step up for creative work, media viewing, and immersive content. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I turn off Copilot/AI features? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. On Surface devices running Windows 11 Pro, you can manage Copilot and AI experiences in Windows Settings, including hiding Copilot from the taskbar and adjusting privacy settings for your device. You can choose which experiences you use, so you stay in control. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "When is Recall going to be available? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Recall is available on Copilot+ PCs. The Pro 13-inch Keyboard is designed to stay attached, delivering a stable, laptop like typing experience for focused work. Both include backlit keys, precision trackpads, and built in pen storage to match different work styles."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I check if an accessory is compatible with my device?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "First, find your device model in Settings > System > About (Surface users can also check the Surface app). "What is a Copilot+ PC? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "A Copilot+ PC is a PC built for AI experiences, with a powerful NPU that can run AI tasks on the device. This helps enable faster, more efficient AI features while keeping work moving smoothly, even when you’re multitasking or on the go."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What AI laptops have enterprise-grade encryption and endpoint protection built in? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft Surface for Business, including Surface Laptop for Business and Surface Pro for Business, are Copilot+ PCs that deliver built-in laptop encryption and endpoint protection for enterprises. TPM 2.0 and BitLocker help encrypt data, while Windows Secure Boot, Secured-core PC, Windows Hello, and Microsoft Defender protect endpoints from chip to cloud, helping safeguard devices, data, and identities. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Where to buy business laptops with financing options? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "You can buy Surface business laptops directly from Microsoft Store. Availability of business laptop financing and payment options may vary by region and seller, allowing organizations to choose purchasing options that fit their budget and procurement needs. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the difference between Intel & Qualcomm Snapdragon® processors? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Intel and Qualcomm Snapdragon® processors differ mainly in architecture and efficiency. Intel Core Ultra processors are widely used in traditional Windows PCs and are known for broad software compatibility and performance. Qualcomm Snapdragon® processors use ARM technology, prioritizing power efficiency and battery life. Both run Windows, but they can deliver different performance, battery, and feature experiences depending on the device."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I turn off Copilot/AI features? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. On Surface devices running Windows 11 Pro, you can manage Copilot and AI experiences in Windows Settings, including hiding Copilot from the taskbar and adjusting privacy settings for your device. You can choose which experiences you use, so you stay in control. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "When is Recall going to be available? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Recall is available on Copilot+ PCs. If you’re shopping, confirm the device is labeled Copilot+ PC, since standard Windows 11 PCs don’t include Recall. Availability can vary by market/region, and you may need the latest Windows updates to see it on your device. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What are the differences between consumer and “for business” devices? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Consumer Surface devices are designed for personal use, while Surface for Business models are built for work environments and come with Windows 11 Pro preinstalled. Business devices may also offer business‑only configurations such as 5G, extended warranty and service options, and enhanced support for security and IT management, including easier deployment, device controls, and long‑term support for organizations."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "If I need to get my device repaired, how do I get support? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Contact Microsoft Support to troubleshoot the issue, check your warranty coverage, and submit a repair or service request. From there, you can select your device, review service options, and track repair status or order details to get back up and running quickly. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I check if an accessory is compatible with my device? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "First, find your device model in  Settings > System > About  (Surface users can also check the Surface app). Then review the accessory’s product page under Specs, Compatibility, or Works with to confirm it supports your model. For some accessories, also check fit (screen size) or connection type (USB-C, Bluetooth, etc.). "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Do you offer flexible payments? How do I use them? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. Microsoft Store offers flexible payment options for eligible purchases, including installment plans and pay-over-time options where available. To use one, select an eligible item, add it to your cart, and—if offered—choose a payment plan during checkout to split your purchase into manageable payments. "}}]}, {"@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "Product", "name": "Surface Laptop for Business, 13-inch | Intel", "url": "https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/d/surface-laptop-for-business-13-inch-intel/8mzbmmcjzpb1", "image": "https://cdn-dynmedia-1.microsoft.com/is/image/microsoftcorp/b00-Surface-Laptop-Intel-13-inch-1Ed-GC-Front-COMMR", "description": "The most portable Surface Laptop delivers built-in AI experiences and impressive performance powered by Intel® Core™ Ultra Series 3 processors. "What is a Copilot+ PC?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "A Copilot+ PC is a PC built for AI experiences, with a powerful NPU that can run AI tasks on the device. This helps enable faster, more efficient AI features while keeping work moving smoothly, even when you’re multitasking or on the go."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What AI laptops have enterprise-grade encryption and endpoint protection built in?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft Surface for Business, including Surface Laptop for Business and Surface Pro for Business, are Copilot+ PCs that deliver built-in laptop encryption and endpoint protection for enterprises. TPM 2.0 and BitLocker help encrypt data, while Windows Secure Boot, Secured-core PC, Windows Hello, and Microsoft Defender protect endpoints from chip to cloud, helping safeguard devices, data, and identities."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Where to buy business laptops with financing options?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "You can buy Surface business laptops directly from  Microsoft Store. Availability of business laptop financing and payment options may vary by region and seller, allowing organizations to choose purchasing options that fit their budget and procurement needs."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the difference between Intel & Qualcomm processors?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Intel and Qualcomm processors differ mainly in architecture and efficiency. Intel Core Ultra processors are widely used in traditional Windows PCs and are known for broad software compatibility and performance. Qualcomm Snapdragon® processors use ARM technology, prioritizing power efficiency and battery life. Both run Windows, but they can deliver different performance, battery, and feature experiences depending on the device."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I turn off Copilot/AI features?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. On Surface devices running Windows 11 Pro, you can manage Copilot and AI experiences in Windows Settings, including hiding Copilot from the taskbar and adjusting privacy settings for your device. You can choose which experiences you use, so you stay in control."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "When is Recall going to be available?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Recall is available on Copilot+ PCs. If you’re shopping, confirm the device is labeled Copilot+ PC, since standard Windows 11 PCs don’t include Recall. Availability can vary by market/region, and you may need the latest Windows updates to see it on your device."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What are the differences between consumer and “for business” devices?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Consumer Surface devices are designed for personal use, while Surface for Business models are built for work environments and come with Windows 11 Pro preinstalled. Business devices may also offer business‑only configurations such as 5G, extended warranty and service options, and enhanced support for security and IT management, including easier deployment, device controls, and long‑term support for organizations."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "If I need to get my device repaired, how do I get support?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Contact Microsoft Support to troubleshoot the issue, check your warranty coverage, and submit a repair or service request. From there, you can select your device, review service options, and track repair status or order details to get back up and running quickly."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I check if an accessory is compatible with my device? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "First, find your device model in Settings > System > About (Surface users can also check the Surface app). Then review the accessory’s product page under Specs, Compatibility, or  Works with to confirm it supports your model. For some accessories, also check fit (screen size) or connection type (USB-C, Bluetooth, etc.)."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Do you offer flexible payments?  How do I use them?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. Microsoft Store offers flexible payment options for eligible purchases, including installment plans and pay-over-time options where available. To use one, select an eligible item, add it to your cart, and—if offered—choose a payment plan during checkout to split your purchase into manageable payments."}}]}, {"@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "Product", "name": "Surface Laptop for Business, Copilot+ PC, 13.8 and 15-inch | Snapdragon", "url": "https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/d/surface-laptop-for-business-copilot-pc-138-and-15-inch-snapdragon/8tkcbz02bdvk", "image": "https://cdn-dynmedia-1.microsoft.com/is/image/microsoftcorp/SPX1B-2000x1500-BB-00", "description": "Surface Laptop for Business, 7ᵗʰ Edition. Intel Core Ultra processors are widely used in traditional Windows PCs and are known for broad software compatibility and performance. Qualcomm Snapdragon® processors use ARM technology, prioritizing power efficiency and battery life. Both run Windows, but they can deliver different performance, battery, and feature experiences depending on the device."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I turn off Copilot/AI features?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. On Surface devices running Windows 11[NM2.1] Pro, you can manage Copilot and AI experiences in Windows Settings, including hiding Copilot from the taskbar and adjusting privacy settings for your device. You can choose which experiences you use, so you stay in control."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "When is Recall going to be available?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Recall is available on Copilot+ PCs. If you’re shopping, confirm the device is labeled Copilot+ PC, since standard Windows 11 PCs don’t include Recall. Availability can vary by market/region, and you may need the latest Windows updates to see it on your device."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I check if the software I use runs on ARM for Windows?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Check the app’s system requirements or product page and look for ARM64 support. Many Windows apps can also run on ARM devices using emulation, but a native ARM64 version may offer improved performance and efficiency. Review Microsoft’s Windows on Arm guidance for more information."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What are the differences between consumer and “for business” devices?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Consumer Surface devices are designed for personal use, while Surface for Business models are built for work environments and come with Windows 11 Pro preinstalled. Business devices may also offer business‑only configurations such as 5G, extended warranty and service options, and enhanced support for security and IT management, including easier deployment, device controls, and long‑term support for organizations."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "If I need to get my device repaired, how do I get support?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Contact Microsoft Support to troubleshoot the issue, check your warranty coverage, and submit a repair or service request. From there, you can select your device, review service options, and track repair status or order details to get back up and running quickly. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I check if an accessory is compatible with my device?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "First, find your device model in Settings > System > About (Surface users can also check the Surface app). Then review the accessory’s product page under Specs, Compatibility, or Works with to confirm it supports your model. For some accessories, also check fit (screen size) or connection type (USB-C, Bluetooth, etc.)."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Do you offer flexible payments? How do I use them?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. Microsoft Store offers flexible payment options for eligible purchases, including installment plans and pay-over-time options where available. [{"@type": "Question", "name": "What happens to my PC when Windows 10 hits end of support (EOS)?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

As of October 14, 2025, PCs running Windows 10 will still function, but Microsoft will no longer provide the following:

  • Technical support
  • Feature updates
  • Free security updates or fixes
     

While your Windows PC will continue to function, it will be at greater risk for viruses and malware when Windows 10 reaches end of support. We recommend you transition to a version of Windows that is still supported. If your existing device cannot run Windows 11 Pro, a new business PC that can run Windows 11 Pro makes for an easy transition and great experience.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Will my Windows 10 PC stop working?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No. Your PC will continue to work, but support for Windows 10 Pro is discontinued. After October 14, 2025, your Windows 10 PC will no longer receive free security updates and Microsoft will no longer provide Windows 10 Pro technical support. Shop now for a Surface for Business PC with Windows 11 Pro."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the best laptop for business? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "For small business needs, consider the Surface Laptop for Business 7th Edition, Copilot+ PC. Another excellent option is the Surface Laptop 6 for Business with Intel® Core™ Ultra processors, AI-driven tools, enhanced security, long battery life, and Windows 11 Pro, available in 13.5- and 15-inch models."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Is Microsoft Surface a tablet or laptop? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft Surface lineup offers versatile devices that can function as both tablets and laptops. To purchase Surface devices, accessories, and Microsoft 365 Business subscriptions at exclusive business pricing through the Microsoft Store for Business you will need:

  • A work account or business email address from your organization.
  • A valid payment method.
  • A shipping address in the United States (for hardware purchases).


To get started visit Small Business Solutions.

It only takes a moment to create a work account. If you already have one, sign in to your work account.

Enter your work email address and complete your organization's sign-in process. Once signed in, you will be able to shop eligible products at exclusive business pricing.

If you don't have a work account, and prefer to purchase with a personal account or as a guest you can continue shopping through our normal experience where both personal and business products are available for purchase at the Microsoft Store.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is a work account?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

A work account allows you to manage your business purchases separately from your personal items and lets you access exclusive business pricing at the Microsoft Store. It's the same account you use for Microsoft 365, Teams, or Outlook at work.

It only takes a moment to create a work account. If you already have one, sign in to your work account.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I sign up for a work account?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "A work account is free and signing up requires just your email, a US-based address, and to put a credit card on file. Sign up for a free work account."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What’s the difference between a work account and MSA?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "An MSA (Microsoft Services Account key) is a personal Microsoft account. Work accounts are for business customers who have signed up for our small business experience."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Where can I find my order history, status of my order, and track my packages?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

You can find the status of your order, see tracking details, and other order details through your order history page. You can find the order status page a few ways based on how you purchased with Microsoft:

  • If you purchased while signed into a work account, go to your Microsoft Store for Business Order History page and login with the same work account used at the time of purchase to see your past purchases.
  • If you purchased while signed in with your personal account, go to Microsoft Account Order History page and login with the same personal account used at the time of purchase to see your past purchases.
  • If you checked out as a guest (your order number starts with “G”), use the Guest Order Lookup to view, track, cancel, or return your order.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I request a return and/or refund?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Microsoft Store (including business purchases) lets you return most physical products within 60 days from the day you receive them, as long as they’re in like-new condition, in the original packaging, with all parts and documentation included. Some products (for example Surface Hub, HoloLens, Windows Dev Kit) are excluded or have different rules.

Start a return from your order history; if an item is eligible, you’ll see a “Request a return” button.

  • If you purchased while signed into a work account, go to your Microsoft Store for Business Order History page and login with the same work account used at the time of purchase to see your past purchases and manage your order.
  • If you purchased while signed in with your personal account, go to Microsoft Account Order History page and login with the same personal account used at the time of purchase to see your past purchases and manage your order.
  • If you checked out as a guest (your order number starts with “G”), use the Guest Order Lookup to view, track, cancel, or return your order.


You can print a prepaid shipping label and send the item back at no shipping cost to you.

Once received and verified, refunds are usually processed to your original payment method within 3–5 business days.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I cancel my order?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

You can try to cancel a Microsoft Store order through your order history page, as long as it hasn’t already shipped or entered the final processing stage.  If the order is still eligible, you’ll see an option like Cancel item or Cancel order, select it and follow the prompts.

If you don’t see a cancel option, the order likely can’t be canceled. In this case, you can let it deliver and then start a return instead via the same order history page.

  • If you purchased while signed into a work account , go to your Microsoft Store for Business Order History page and login with the same work account used to purchase.
  • If you purchased while signed in with your personal account, go to Microsoft Account Order History page and login with the same personal account used to purchase to see your past purchases.
  • If you checked out as a guest (your order number starts with “G”), use the Guest Order Lookup.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How I find my tax invoice?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

For business purchases, tax invoices are usually available either in your Microsoft Store business order history or in the Microsoft 365 admin center, depending on where/what you bought. Make sure you are signed in with the same account you used to make the purchases.

For Microsoft 365 or other subscription billing, use the admin center:

  1. Sign in to the Microsoft 365 admin center at admin.microsoft.com.
  2. Go to Billing > Bills & payments to view and download tax invoices for your organization. 


For Store hardware/one‑time purchases:

  1. Go to Business order history: View your business order history and sign in with the same work account you used to purchase.
  2. Open the relevant order; if a tax invoice is available, you’ll see an option to View invoice or Download invoice/receipt as a PDF.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is Microsoft OneDrive?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

OneDrive is a Microsoft cloud storage service that lets you store your personal files in one place, share them with others, and access them from any device connected to the internet. Learn more about OneDrive.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I add a Microsoft account to OneDrive?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Learn how to add accounts in OneDrive.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How much cloud storage is available on my Microsoft account?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

A Microsoft account includes 5 GB of cloud storage with OneDrive. If you need more space for your files, you can upgrade to a qualifying Microsoft 365 subscription, which offers additional storage and premium features. See available options.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I transfer my files using an external storage device?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Yes, you can bring your files with you using an external storage device such as a USB drive, SD card, or external hard drive. Learn how.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I use Microsoft Office on a new computer?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Yes. You can install or activate compatible versions of Office on your new computer using your product key.

Activate Office on a new PC >

How to install Office >

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I transfer older programs and applications to a new Windows computer?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Windows Backup helps you move files, settings, and a list of your apps when setting up a new Windows 11 PC. It works with PCs running Windows 10 or later and restores on Windows 11, version 22H2 or higher.

Keep in mind that apps themselves aren’t automatically transferred or installed. When you open an app for the first time on your new PC, some can be reinstalled directly from the Microsoft Store. Others may need to be reinstalled manually from the original provider. Geographic restrictions may apply. See WOA for Arm-compatible apps and Windows Backup for details.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I trade in my older Windows 10 laptop or PC for an upgrade?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Microsoft Store offers a Trade-in Program powered by Teladvance in select countries. You may be eligible for cash back if your device meets the criteria for offers currently available in your country. Just answer a few questions about your device’s brand, model, and condition to see if it qualifies. Get started by checking your device’s estimated trade-in value. Available trade-in offers and eligibility requirements apply and may vary over time.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the Windows 10 Extended Security Updates (ESU) program?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Microsoft announced last October the Extended Security Updates (ESU) consumer program that gives the option to receive critical and important security updates for eligible Windows 10 PCs enrolled in the program through October 13, 2026. The program provides you with more time to stay protected on your current Windows 10 device before moving to a new Surface PC.

For additional information on Consumer ESU options, please visit ESU Settings or the ESU support page.

For additional information on Commercial ESU program, please visit the Microsoft Learn page.

"}}]}, {"@type": "FAQPage", "@context": "http://schema.org", "mainEntity": [{"@type": "Question", "name": "What is a work account?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "A work account allows you to manage your business purchases separately from your personal items and lets you access exclusive business pricing at the Microsoft Store. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Do I need Microsoft 365 to shop for my small business?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

[{"@type": "Question", "name": "What happens to my PC when Windows 10 hits end of support (EOS)?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

As of October 14, 2025, PCs running Windows 10 will still function, but Microsoft will no longer provide the following:

  • Technical support
  • Feature updates
  • Free security updates or fixes
     

While your Windows PC will continue to function, it will be at greater risk for viruses and malware when Windows 10 reaches end of support. We recommend you transition to a version of Windows that is still supported. If your existing device cannot run Windows 11 Pro, a new business PC that can run Windows 11 Pro makes for an easy transition and great experience.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Will my Windows 10 PC stop working?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No. Your PC will continue to work, but support for Windows 10 Pro is discontinued. Intel Core Ultra processors are widely used in traditional Windows PCs and are known for broad software compatibility and performance. Qualcomm Snapdragon® processors use ARM technology, prioritizing power efficiency and battery life. Both run Windows, but they can deliver different performance, battery, and feature experiences depending on the device."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I turn off Copilot/AI features? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. On Surface devices running Windows 11 Pro, you can manage Copilot and AI experiences in Windows Settings, including hiding Copilot from the taskbar and adjusting privacy settings for your device. You can choose which experiences you use, so you stay in control. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "When is Recall going to be available? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Recall is available on Copilot+ PCs. ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Consumer Surface devices are designed for personal use, while Surface for Business models are built for work environments and come with Windows 11 Pro preinstalled. Business devices may also offer business‑only configurations such as 5G, extended warranty and service options, and enhanced support for security and IT management, including easier deployment, device controls, and long‑term support for organizations."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "If I need to get my device repaired, how do I get support? ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Contact Microsoft Support to troubleshoot the issue, check your warranty coverage, and submit a repair or service request. ", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "First, find your device model in  Settings > System > About  (Surface users can also check the Surface app). [{"@type": "Question", "name": "What happens to my PC when Windows 10 hits end of support (EOS)?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

As of October 14, 2025, PCs running Windows 10 will still function, but Microsoft will no longer provide the following:

  • Technical support
  • Feature updates
  • Free security updates or fixes
     

While your Windows PC will continue to function, it will be at greater risk for viruses and malware when Windows 10 reaches end of support. We recommend you transition to a version of Windows that is still supported. If your existing device cannot run Windows 11 Pro, a new business PC that can run Windows 11 Pro makes for an easy transition and great experience.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Will my Windows 10 PC stop working?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "No. Your PC will continue to work, but support for Windows 10 Pro is discontinued. After October 14, 2025, your Windows 10 PC will no longer receive free security updates and Microsoft will no longer provide Windows 10 Pro technical support. Shop now for a Surface for Business PC with Windows 11 Pro."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the best laptop for business?   • A work account or business email address from your organization.
  • A valid payment method.
  • A shipping address in the United States (for hardware purchases).

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is a work account?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I sign up for a work account?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "A work account is free and signing up requires just your email, a US-based address, and to put a credit card on file. Sign up for a free work account."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What’s the difference between a work account and MSA?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "An MSA (Microsoft Services Account key) is a personal Microsoft account. You can find the status of your order, see tracking details, and other order details through your order history page. You can find the order status page a few ways based on how you purchased with Microsoft:

  • If you purchased while signed into a work account, go to your Microsoft Store for Business Order History page and login with the same work account used at the time of purchase to see your past purchases.
  • If you purchased while signed in with your personal account, go to Microsoft Account Order History page and login with the same personal account used at the time of purchase to see your past purchases.
  • If you checked out as a guest (your order number starts with “G”), use the Guest Order Lookup to view, track, cancel, or return your order.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I request a return and/or refund?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Microsoft Store (including business purchases) lets you return most physical products within 60 days from the day you receive them, as long as they’re in like-new condition, in the original packaging, with all parts and documentation included. Some products (for example Surface Hub, HoloLens, Windows Dev Kit) are excluded or have different rules.

Start a return from your order history; if an item is eligible, you’ll see a “Request a return” button.

  • If you purchased while signed into a work account, go to your Microsoft Store for Business Order History page and login with the same work account used at the time of purchase to see your past purchases and manage your order.
  • If you purchased while signed in with your personal account, go to Microsoft Account Order History page and login with the same personal account used at the time of purchase to see your past purchases and manage your order.
  • If you checked out as a guest (your order number starts with “G”), use the Guest Order Lookup to view, track, cancel, or return your order.


You can print a prepaid shipping label and send the item back at no shipping cost to you.

Once received and verified, refunds are usually processed to your original payment method within 3–5 business days.

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How do I cancel my order?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

You can try to cancel a Microsoft Store order through your order history page, as long as it hasn’t already shipped or entered the final processing stage.  If the order is still eligible, you’ll see an option like Cancel item or Cancel order, select it and follow the prompts.

If you don’t see a cancel option, the order likely can’t be canceled. In this case, you can let it deliver and then start a return instead via the same order history page.

  • If you purchased while signed into a work account , go to your Microsoft Store for Business Order History page and login with the same work account used to purchase.
  • If you purchased while signed in with your personal account, go to Microsoft Account Order History page and login with the same personal account used to purchase to see your past purchases.
  • If you checked out as a guest (your order number starts with “G”), use the Guest Order Lookup.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How I find my tax invoice?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

For business purchases, tax invoices are usually available either in your Microsoft Store business order history or in the Microsoft 365 admin center, depending on where/what you bought. Make sure you are signed in with the same account you used to make the purchases.

For Microsoft 365 or other subscription billing, use the admin center:

  1. Sign in to the Microsoft 365 admin center at admin.microsoft.com.
  2. Go to Billing > Bills & payments to view and download tax invoices for your organization. 


For Store hardware/one‑time purchases:

  1. Go to Business order history: View your business order history and sign in with the same work account you used to purchase.
  2. Open the relevant order; if a tax invoice is available, you’ll see an option to View invoice or Download invoice/receipt as a PDF.
"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I keep my company's private information secure?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Microsoft Security for small business provides cost-effective solutions against security breaches. "What is a Copilot+ PC?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "A Copilot+ PC is a PC built for AI experiences, with a powerful NPU that can run AI tasks on the device. This helps enable faster, more efficient AI features while keeping work moving smoothly, even when you’re multitasking or on the go."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Does Surface come with 5G?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "

Yes. Some Surface models come with 5G, but not all Surface devices include cellular connectivity. If you want 5G, make sure you choose a configuration that specifically lists 5G support. 

5G-enabled Surface devices work with a mobile data plan using either a physical SIM card (nano SIM) or eSIM, depending on the model. 

"}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Where to buy business Pros with financing options?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "You can buy Surface business Pros directly from Microsoft Store. Availability of business laptop financing and payment options may vary by region and seller, allowing organizations to choose purchasing options that fit their budget and procurement needs."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the best Surface Pro for business and personal use?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Surface Pro for Business  stands out as one of the top laptops for business and personal use, delivering powerful performance, ultra-portability, and enterprise-grade security in a versatile 2-in-1 design[CQ4.1][KH4.2][SI4.3]. Built for professionals on the go, it seamlessly replaces both a tablet and a laptop. Powered by Intel® Core™ Ultra processors and Windows 11 Pro, it supports touch, pen, and keyboard workflows with reliable, manageable performance for work and everyday tasks."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What are good lightweight business laptops with Microsoft Office and productivity tools?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Surface Pro for Business is a lightweight business laptop that’s easy to bring anywhere. It comes with Windows 11 Pro and runs Microsoft Office apps and other productivity tools, so you can stay on top of work without being weighed down. With its portable 2-in-1 design and strong performance, it’s a great option if you’re looking for a lightweight business laptop["}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the difference between Intel & Qualcomm Snapdragon processors?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Intel and Qualcomm processors differ mainly in architecture and efficiency. Intel Core Ultra processors are widely used in traditional Windows PCs and are known for broad software compatibility and performance. Qualcomm Snapdragon® processors use ARM technology, prioritizing power efficiency and battery life. Both run Windows, but they can deliver different performance, battery, and feature experiences depending on the device. "}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What is the difference between LCD and OLED?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "LCD (Liquid Crystal Display) screens use a backlight to display images with consistent brightness and sharp resolution, making them well suited for everyday tasks like email, web browsing, document work, and video calls. OLED (Organic Light Emitting Diode) screens light each pixel individually to display images with deeper blacks, higher contrast, and support for HDR, making them a step up for creative work, media viewing, and immersive content."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Can I turn off Copilot/AI features?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. On Surface devices running Windows 11 Pro, you can manage Copilot and AI experiences in Windows Settings, including hiding Copilot from the taskbar and adjusting privacy settings for your device. You can choose which experiences you use, so you stay in control."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "When is Recall going to be available?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Recall is available on Copilot+ PCs. If you’re shopping, confirm the device is labeled Copilot+ PC, since standard Windows 11 PCs don’t include Recall. Availability can vary by market/region, and you may need the latest Windows updates to see it on your device."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "What are the differences between consumer and “for business” devices?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Consumer Surface devices are designed for personal use, while Surface for Business models are built for work environments and come with Windows 11 Pro preinstalled. Business devices may also offer business‑only configurations such as 5G, extended warranty and service options, and enhanced support for security and IT management, including easier deployment, device controls, and long‑term support for organizations."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "If I need to get my device repaired, how do I get support?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Contact Microsoft Support to troubleshoot the issue, check your warranty coverage, and submit a repair or service request. From there, you can select your device, review service options, and track repair status or order details to get back up and running quickly."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Which keyboard is right for my device?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Surface Pro supports two detachable keyboard options. The Pro 13-inch Flex Keyboard works attached or detached, giving you greater freedom to present, sketch, or move around while staying productive. The Pro 13-inch Keyboard is designed to stay attached, delivering a stable, laptop like typing experience for focused work. Both include backlit keys, precision trackpads, and built in pen storage to match different work styles."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "How can I check if an accessory is compatible with my device?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "First, find your device model in Settings > System > About (Surface users can also check the Surface app). Then review the accessory’s product page under Specs, Compatibility, or Works with to confirm it supports your model. For some accessories, also check fit (screen size) or connection type (USB-C, Bluetooth, etc.)."}}, {"@type": "Question", "name": "Do you offer flexible payments? How do I use them?", "acceptedAnswer": {"@type": "Answer", "text": "Yes. Microsoft Store offers flexible payment options for eligible purchases, including installment plans and pay-over-time options where available.
